title: "Air_Pollution_Research_V1_public"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-17"
---

Portfolio public-release note: this R Markdown file preserves the original exploratory and modeling workflow, but the raw household-level data are not included in this repository. Local machine paths were replaced with relative placeholders such as `data/private/` and `results/`.

### Library
```{r}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)

```


Set working dictionary
```{r}
df <- read_excel("data/private/IndoorAirQuality_full_raw_data_new.xlsx")
```

Comparison and grouping from the previous code
```{r}

#Village
focus_villages <- c("Mohuldiya", "Nelung", "Sankarpur","Jharbelda")
df$focus_group <- ifelse(df$Village %in% focus_villages, "Focus", "Regular")

#Education
# Reclassify education levels with combined categories
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Education_Grouped = case_when(
    `Q1F Education` %in% c(0, 1) ~ "No Formal Education",
    `Q1F Education` %in% c(2, 3) ~ "Primary or Middle School",
    `Q1F Education` %in% c(4, 5, 6, 7) ~ "High School and Above"
  )) %>%
  mutate(Education_Grouped = factor(Education_Grouped, levels = c(
    "No Formal Education",
    "Primary or Middle School",
    "High School and Above"
  )))



#Electricity
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Electricity_Grouped = case_when(
    `Q1P Electricity` == 2 ~ "No Electricity",  
    `Q1P Electricity` == 1 ~ "Has Electricity",  
    is.na(`Q1P Electricity`) ~ "Unknown/Missing",  # Handle missing values explicitly
    TRUE ~ "Other"  # Catch any unexpected values
  ))

# Convert to factor with ordered levels
df$Electricity_Grouped <- factor(df$Electricity_Grouped, 
                                 levels = c("No Electricity", "Has Electricity"))


#Income
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Income_Grouped = case_when(
    `Q1J avg fam income` == 1             ~ "Less than Rs 5000",
    `Q1J avg fam income` == 2             ~ "Rs 5000 – 10,000",
    `Q1J avg fam income` %in% c(3, 4)     ~ "More than Rs 10,000",
    TRUE                                  ~ NA_character_  # excludes 5 or NA
  )) %>%
  mutate(Income_Grouped = factor(Income_Grouped, levels = c(
    "Less than Rs 5000",
    "Rs 5000 – 10,000",
    "More than Rs 10,000"
  )))

#Roof Material
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Roof_Category = case_when(
      `Q4L roof material` %in% c(1, 3, 4) ~ "Non-Cement",  # Groups 1 (Khappar), 3 (Thatched), and 4 (Others)
      `Q4L roof material` == 2 ~ "Cement",                 # Group 2 (Cement tile),maybe wrost off check graphs
      TRUE ~ NA_character_                                  # Assigns NA if there are unexpected values
    )
  )

#Kitchen Ventilation
  # Create a new ventilation category variable
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Ventilation_Category = case_when(
    `Q4F open eves` == 2 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 2 ~ "No-No",
    `Q4F open eves` == 2 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 1 ~ "No-Yes",
    `Q4F open eves` == 1 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 2 ~ "Yes-No",
    `Q4F open eves` == 1 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 1 ~ "Yes-Yes",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Handle missing values
  ))

  # Convert to an ordered factor for better comparisons
df$Kitchen_Ventilation_Category <- factor(df$Kitchen_Ventilation_Category, 
                                          levels = c("No-No", "No-Yes", "Yes-No", "Yes-Yes"))

#Smoke Exposure
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination = paste(
      `Q5A use incense`,
      `Q5B mosquito coils`,
      `Q5C keros lantern`,
      sep = "-"
    ),
    
    # Assign categories
    Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination = case_when(
      Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination %in% c("1-1-1", "1-1-2", "1-2-1", "2-1-1",
                               "1-1-3", "1-2-2", "2-1-2", "2-2-1") ~ "High Use",
      
      Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination %in% c("1-2-3", "1-3-1", "2-1-3", "2-2-2",
                               "2-3-1", "3-1-1", "3-1-2", "3-2-1", "3-2-2") ~ "Medium Use",
      
      TRUE ~ "Low Use"
    )
  )

#Indoor_Outdoor Kitchen Location
# Mapping values for Improved and Traditional cookstoves
df$indoor_outdoor_kitchen <- factor(df$`Q4A kitchen location`, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 6),
                                     labels = c('Indoor', 'Indoor', 'Indoor', 'Outdoor', 'Outdoor'))


# Household Size
df$HH_Size_Grouped <- case_when(
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "Small (1-3)",
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` %in% c(4, 5, 6) ~ "Medium (4-6)",
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` >= 7 ~ "Large (7+)",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Preserve NA values
)

# Wheezing HQ5A
df$Wheezing <- ifelse(df$HQ5A_apart == 1 | 
                                 df$HQ5A_cold == 1 | 
                                 df$HQ5A_most == 1, 1, 0)



#4/25/2025 New indoor and outdoor grouping (this is just testing)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Location_Category = case_when(
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 1             ~ "Kitchen in Living Area",
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 2             ~ "Separate Indoor Kitchen",
    `Q4A kitchen location` %in% c(3, 4)     ~ "Semi-Outdoor Kitchen",
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 6             ~ "Detached Outdoor Kitchen",
    TRUE                                    ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Location_Category = factor(Kitchen_Location_Category, levels = c(
    "Kitchen in Living Area",
    "Separate Indoor Kitchen",
    "Semi-Outdoor Kitchen",
    "Detached Outdoor Kitchen"
  )))
```

Create ATS score for cough (Question 2), phlegm (Question 3), Dyspnea (Question 6)

Cough (Question 2)
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Function to assign cough scores (sum approach) and categorize responses
assign_cough_score_summary_sum <- function(data) {
  # Initialize columns
  data$cough_score <- NA
  data$cough_category <- NA

  # Skip 2B and 2C if 2A = No
  data$`HQ2B high frequent cough 4d+/week`[data$`HQ2A cough regular` == 2] <- NA
  data$`HQ2C morning cough`[data$`HQ2A cough regular` == 2] <- NA
  
  # Count the number of "Yes" (1) responses
  data$cough_score <- rowSums(
    cbind(
      as.numeric(data$`HQ2A cough regular` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ2B high frequent cough 4d+/week` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ2C morning cough` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ2D day/night cough` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ2E persistent cough for 5m+/yr` == 1)
    ), 
    na.rm = TRUE # Ignore NA values in the summation
  )

  # Assign cough categories based on score
  data$cough_category <- case_when(
    rowSums(!is.na(data[, c(
      "HQ2A cough regular",
      "HQ2B high frequent cough 4d+/week",
      "HQ2C morning cough",
      "HQ2D day/night cough",
      "HQ2E persistent cough for 5m+/yr"
    )])) == 0 ~ "Missing",             # All responses are NA
    data$cough_score == 0 ~ "No Cough",                        # Combine "No Cough" and "NA"
    data$cough_score %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Frequent Cough",          # Mild symptoms
    data$cough_score >= 3 ~ "Chronic Cough"                    # Severe symptoms
  )
  
  # Reorder cough categories (explicitly include "Missing")
  data$cough_category <- factor(
    data$cough_category, 
    levels = c("No Cough", "Frequent Cough", "Chronic Cough", "Missing")
  )
  
  # Adjust cough_score to separate Missing as 88
  data$cough_score[data$cough_category == "Missing"] <- 88
  
  # Reorder Cough Score Distribution
  score_levels <- c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 88)  # Add 88 as the last number
  score_dist <- table(factor(data$cough_score, levels = score_levels), useNA = "ifany")
  
  # Display results
  cat("\n=== Cough Score Distribution (Reordered with 88 for Missing) ===\n")
  print(score_dist)
  
  cat("\n=== Cough Category Distribution (Combined) ===\n")
  print(table(data$cough_category, useNA = "ifany"))
  
  # Return modified dataset
  return(data)
}

# Apply the function to your dataset
df <- assign_cough_score_summary_sum(df)

# View the first few rows of the updated dataset
print(head(df))
```


Phlegm (Question 3)
```{r}
# Function to assign phlegm scores (sum approach) and categorize responses
assign_phlegm_score_summary_sum <- function(data) {
  # Initialize columns
  data$phlegm_score <- NA
  data$phlegm_category <- NA

  # Skip HQ3B and HQ3C if HQ3A = No
  data$`HQ3B high phlegm frequency 4d+/week`[data$`HQ3A phlegm regular` == 2] <- NA
  data$`HQ3C morning phlegm`[data$`HQ3A phlegm regular` == 2] <- NA
  
  # Skip HQ3E and HQ3F if "No" to all of HQ3A–HQ3D
  any_yes_abcd <- (
    data$`HQ3A phlegm regular` == 1 |
    data$`HQ3B high phlegm frequency 4d+/week` == 1 |
    data$`HQ3C morning phlegm` == 1 |
    data$`HQ3D day/night phlegm` == 1
  )
  data$`HQ3E persistent phlegm 3m+/yr`[!any_yes_abcd] <- NA
  data$`HQ3F years with phlegm`[!any_yes_abcd] <- NA

  # Treat "Does Not Apply" (8) as NA for HQ3E and HQ3F
  data$`HQ3E persistent phlegm 3m+/yr`[data$`HQ3E persistent phlegm 3m+/yr` == 8] <- NA
  data$`HQ3F years with phlegm`[data$`HQ3F years with phlegm` == 88] <- NA

  # Count the number of "Yes" (1) responses
  data$phlegm_score <- rowSums(
    cbind(
      as.numeric(data$`HQ3A phlegm regular` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ3B high phlegm frequency 4d+/week` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ3C morning phlegm` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ3D day/night phlegm` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ3E persistent phlegm 3m+/yr` == 1)
    ), 
    na.rm = TRUE # Ignore NA values in the summation
  )

  # Assign phlegm categories based on score
  data$phlegm_category <- case_when(
    rowSums(!is.na(data[, c(
      "HQ3A phlegm regular",
      "HQ3B high phlegm frequency 4d+/week",
      "HQ3C morning phlegm",
      "HQ3D day/night phlegm",
      "HQ3E persistent phlegm 3m+/yr",
      "HQ3F years with phlegm"
    )])) == 0 ~ "Missing",             # All responses are NA
    data$phlegm_score == 0 ~ "No Phlegm",                        # Combine "No Phlegm" and "NA"
    data$phlegm_score %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Frequent Phlegm",          # Mild symptoms
    data$phlegm_score >= 3 ~ "Chronic Phlegm"                    # Severe symptoms
  )
  
  # Reorder phlegm categories (explicitly include "Missing")
  data$phlegm_category <- factor(
    data$phlegm_category, 
    levels = c("No Phlegm", "Frequent Phlegm", "Chronic Phlegm", "Missing")
  )
  
  # Adjust phlegm_score to separate Missing as 88
  data$phlegm_score[data$phlegm_category == "Missing"] <- 88
  
  # Reorder Phlegm Score Distribution
  score_levels <- c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 88)  # Add 88 as the last number
  score_dist <- table(factor(data$phlegm_score, levels = score_levels), useNA = "ifany")
  
  # Display results
  cat("\n=== Phlegm Score Distribution (Reordered with 88 for Missing) ===\n")
  print(score_dist)
  
  cat("\n=== Phlegm Category Distribution (Combined) ===\n")
  print(table(data$phlegm_category, useNA = "ifany"))
  
  # Return modified dataset
  return(data)
}

# Apply the function to your dataset
df <- assign_phlegm_score_summary_sum(df)

# View the first few rows of the updated dataset
print(head(df))

```



Dyspnea (Question 6)
```{r}
# Function to assign breathlessness scores (sum approach) and categorize responses
assign_breathlessness_score_summary_sum <- function(data) {
  # Initialize columns
  data$breathlessness_score <- NA
  data$breathlessness_category <- NA

  # Count the number of "Yes" (1) responses
  data$breathlessness_score <- rowSums(
    cbind(
      as.numeric(data$`HQ6B shortness of breath on slight exertion` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ6C slower pace (breathlessness condition)` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ6D stops walking for breath ` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ6E stops for breath after 100 yards` == 1),
      as.numeric(data$`HQ6F breathless at home or dressing` == 1)
    ), 
    na.rm = TRUE # Ignore NA values in the summation
  )

  # Assign breathlessness categories based on score
  data$breathlessness_category <- case_when(
    rowSums(!is.na(data[, c(
      "HQ6B shortness of breath on slight exertion",
      "HQ6C slower pace (breathlessness condition)",
      "HQ6D stops walking for breath ",
      "HQ6E stops for breath after 100 yards",
      "HQ6F breathless at home or dressing"
    )])) == 0 ~ "Missing",             # All responses are NA
    data$breathlessness_score == 0 ~ "No Breathlessness",       # Combine "No Breathlessness" and "NA"
    data$breathlessness_score %in% c(1, 2) ~ "Mild Breathlessness", # Mild symptoms
    data$breathlessness_score >= 3 ~ "Severe Breathlessness"        # Severe symptoms
  )
  
  # Reorder breathlessness categories (explicitly include "Missing")
  data$breathlessness_category <- factor(
    data$breathlessness_category, 
    levels = c("No Breathlessness", "Mild Breathlessness", "Severe Breathlessness", "Missing")
  )
  
  # Adjust breathlessness_score to separate Missing as 88
  data$breathlessness_score[data$breathlessness_category == "Missing"] <- 88
  
  # Reorder Breathlessness Score Distribution
  score_levels <- c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 88)  # Add 88 as the last number
  score_dist <- table(factor(data$breathlessness_score, levels = score_levels), useNA = "ifany")
  
  # Display results
  cat("\n=== Breathlessness Score Distribution (Reordered with 88 for Missing) ===\n")
  print(score_dist)
  
  cat("\n=== Breathlessness Category Distribution (Combined) ===\n")
  print(table(data$breathlessness_category, useNA = "ifany"))
  
  # Return modified dataset
  return(data)
}

# Apply the function to your dataset
df <- assign_breathlessness_score_summary_sum(df)

# View the first few rows of the updated dataset
print(head(df))
```


Frequency and Percentage for All Variable
```{r,include=FALSE}
cross_tabulate_with_cookstove <- function(data, cookstove_col = "Type", max_unique_values = 10, max_na_count = 30) {
  # Define the original variables to skip because log-transformed versions will be used later
  log_vars <- c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
                "PM10_c", "PM2.5_c", "PM1_c", "BC_c", "CO_c")
  
  # Ensure the cookstove column is a factor for grouping
  data[[cookstove_col]] <- as.factor(data[[cookstove_col]])
  
  # Initialize vectors to store skipped or significant variables
  skipped_vars_unique <- c()
  skipped_vars_na <- c()
  vars_with_dependency <- c()
  vars_with_dependency_and_high_na <- c()
  vars_with_difference <- c()
  
  # Loop through each column in the dataset
  for (col_name in colnames(data)) {
    # Skip the cookstove column itself and any manually skipped variables
    if (col_name == cookstove_col || col_name %in% log_vars) next
    
    # Count NA values
    na_count <- sum(is.na(data[[col_name]]))
    
    # Skip variable if too many NAs
    if (na_count > max_na_count) {
      skipped_vars_na <- c(skipped_vars_na, col_name)
      next
    }
    
    # Check number of unique values
    num_unique_values <- length(unique(na.omit(data[[col_name]])))
    
    # Skip categorical cross-tab if too many unique values, but still do MWU test if numeric
    if (num_unique_values > max_unique_values) {
      skipped_vars_unique <- c(skipped_vars_unique, col_name)
      
      if (is.numeric(data[[col_name]])) {
        test_result <- wilcox.test(data[[col_name]] ~ data[[cookstove_col]], na.action = na.omit)
        if (test_result$p.value < 0.05) {
          vars_with_difference <- c(vars_with_difference, col_name)
        }
      }
      next
    }
    
    # Cross-tabulation with percentages
    table_data <- table(data[[cookstove_col]], addNA(data[[col_name]]), useNA = "ifany")
    table_data_percent <- prop.table(table_data, margin = 1) * 100
    table_with_percent <- paste0(table_data, " (", round(table_data_percent, 1), "%)")
    table_with_percent <- matrix(table_with_percent, nrow = nrow(table_data), dimnames = dimnames(table_data))
    
    # Print table
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Cross-tabulation for:", col_name, "vs", cookstove_col, "\n")
    cat("-------------------------------------------\n")
    print(table_with_percent)
    
    # Dependency check (Chi-square or Fisher’s test)
    table_data_no_na <- table(data[[cookstove_col]], data[[col_name]], useNA = "no")
    if (sum(table_data_no_na) == 0 || nrow(table_data_no_na) < 2 || ncol(table_data_no_na) < 2) next
    
    expected_counts <- chisq.test(table_data_no_na, simulate.p.value = TRUE)$expected
    meets_expected_count <- all(expected_counts >= 5)
    
    if (meets_expected_count) {
      test_result <- chisq.test(table_data_no_na)
    } else {
      test_result <- fisher.test(table_data_no_na, simulate.p.value = TRUE)
    }
    
    if (test_result$p.value < 0.05) {
      vars_with_dependency <- c(vars_with_dependency, col_name)
      if (na_count > max_na_count) {
        vars_with_dependency_and_high_na <- c(vars_with_dependency_and_high_na, col_name)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Summary of skipped and significant variables
  if (length(skipped_vars_unique) > 0) {
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Skipped variables with too many unique values:\n")
    print(skipped_vars_unique)
  } else {
    cat("\nNo variables were skipped due to unique values.\n")
  }
  
  if (length(skipped_vars_na) > 0) {
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Variables with more than", max_na_count, "NA values:\n")
    print(skipped_vars_na)
  } else {
    cat("\nNo variables have more than", max_na_count, "NA values.\n")
  }
  
  if (length(vars_with_dependency) > 0) {
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Variables with dependency on cookstove type:\n")
    print(vars_with_dependency)
  } else {
    cat("\nNo variables show dependency on cookstove type.\n")
  }
  
  if (length(vars_with_dependency_and_high_na) > 0) {
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Variables with dependency on cookstove type and high NA values:\n")
    print(vars_with_dependency_and_high_na)
  } else {
    cat("\nNo dependent variables have high NA values.\n")
  }
  
  if (length(vars_with_difference) > 0) {
    cat("\n-------------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Continuous variables with significant difference between cookstove types:\n")
    print(vars_with_difference)
  } else {
    cat("\nNo continuous variables show significant difference between cookstove types.\n")
  }
}

cross_tabulate_with_cookstove(df, cookstove_col = "Type", max_unique_values = 10, max_na_count = 90)
```


For the continuous pollutant variables, we suspected—and confirmed—that some were not normally distributed and/or did not meet the equal variance assumption. These violations affect the choice of statistical tests for group comparisons.

Original Distribution
```{r}
#PM10_24hr,PM2.5_24hr,PM1_24hr,CO_24hr,PM10_c,PM2.5_c,PM1_c,BC_c,CO_c

# Load ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

# List of variables to plot
vars <- c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
          "PM10_c", "PM2.5_c", "PM1_c", "BC_c", "CO_c")

# Loop through each variable and create both density plot and QQ plot
for (var in vars) {
  # Density plot
  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes_string(x = var)) +
    geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.6) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Density Plot of", var),
         x = var, y = "Density")
  
  print(p1)
  
  # Q-Q plot
  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes_string(sample = var)) +
    stat_qq() +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Q-Q Plot of", var),
         x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles")
  
  print(p2)
}
```


Log-Transformation Distribution
```{R}
# Load ggplot2
library(ggplot2)

df$kitchen_type <- factor(df$`Q4A kitchen location`, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 6),
                                     labels = c('Indoor_Kitchen', 'Indoor_Kitchen', 'Indoor_Kitchen', 'Outdoor_Kitchen', 'Outdoor_Kitchen'))

# List of variables to log-transform and plot
vars <- c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
          "PM10_c", "PM2.5_c", "PM1_c", "BC_c", "CO_c")

# Create log-transformed columns (adding 1 to avoid log(0))
for (var in vars) {
  df[[paste0("log_", var)]] <- log(df[[var]] + 1)
}

# Plot each log-transformed variable as density plot and Q-Q plot
for (var in vars) {
  log_var <- paste0("log_", var)
  
  # Density plot
  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes_string(x = log_var)) +
    geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.6) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Density Plot of", log_var),
         x = log_var, y = "Density")
  print(p1)
  
  # Q-Q plot
  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes_string(sample = log_var)) +
    stat_qq() +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste("Q-Q Plot of", log_var),
         x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles")
  print(p2)
}


```



Step to apply log transformation to the air pollutant variable, check normality and equal variance, then check the statistical difference between categorical variable

```{R}
combined_group_analysis <- function(df, cont_vars, group_vars,
                                    min_n_per_group  = 15,
                                    min_n_two_groups = 10) {

  if (!requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) stop("Please install {car}")
  if (!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) stop("Please install {ggplot2}")

  detect_type <- function(x) {
    if (is.factor(x) || is.character(x))
      return(if (nlevels(factor(na.omit(x))) == 2) "binary" else "categorical")
    if (is.numeric(x))
      return(if (all(na.omit(x) %in% c(0, 1))) "binary" else "continuous")
    "unknown"
  }

  present_groups <- intersect(group_vars, names(df))
  if (length(miss <- setdiff(group_vars, names(df)))) {
    warning("Skipping missing grouping vars: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))
  }

  out <- data.frame(
    Group                      = character(),
    Variable_Frequency_Display = character(),
    Sample_Size_Passed         = character(),
    Variable                   = character(),
    Comparison_Type            = character(),
    Normality                  = logical(),
    Equal_Variance             = logical(),
    Group_Difference_Test      = character(),
    Group_Difference_P_Value   = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  for (grp in present_groups) {
    df[[grp]] <- factor(df[[grp]])
    grp_type  <- detect_type(df[[grp]])
    nlev      <- nlevels(df[[grp]])

    for (var in cont_vars) {
      print(paste("Currently processing:", var))
      df_sub <- df[complete.cases(df[, c(grp, var)]), c(grp, var)]
      counts <- as.integer(table(df_sub[[grp]]))
      freq_display <- paste(counts, collapse = " vs ")
      
      if (nrow(df_sub) == 0 ||
      nlevels(droplevels(df_sub[[grp]])) < 2) {
    cat("Skipping", var, "for", grp,
        ": not enough data or only one group after filtering\n")
    next
  }


      ## sample‑size rule
      tiny_group <- any(counts < 5)
      if (nlev == 2) {
        ss_pass <- if (tiny_group) "FAIL_small(<5)" else
                   if (all(counts >= min_n_two_groups)) "PASS" else "FAIL"
      } else {
        ss_pass <- if (tiny_group) "FAIL_small(<5)" else
                   if (all(counts >= min_n_per_group)) "PASS" else "FAIL"
      }

      ## normality check within each group (skip group if n<3)
      shaps <- tapply(df_sub[[var]], df_sub[[grp]], function(x)
        if (length(x) >= 3) shapiro.test(x)$p.value else NA_real_)
      is_norm <- all(na.omit(shaps) > 0.05)

      ## variance test (Brown‑Forsythe if non‑normal, classic Levene if normal)
      lev <- car::leveneTest(
        as.formula(paste(var, "~", grp)),
        data = df_sub,
        center = if (is_norm) mean else median)
      is_eq <- lev$`Pr(>F)`[1] > 0.05

      ## choose test
      if (nlev == 2) {                        # ----- TWO GROUPS -----
        if (tiny_group || ss_pass != "PASS") {
          test_name <- "Mann‑Whitney U"
        } else if (is_norm && is_eq) {
          test_name <- "T‑test"
        } else if (is_norm && !is_eq) {
          test_name <- "Welch t"
        } else {
          test_name <- "Mann‑Whitney U"
        }
      } else {                               # ----- ≥3 GROUPS -----
        if (tiny_group || ss_pass != "PASS") {
          test_name <- "Kruskal‑Wallis"
        } else if (is_norm && is_eq) {
          test_name <- "ANOVA"
        } else if (is_norm && !is_eq) {
          test_name <- "Welch ANOVA"
        } else {
          test_name <- "Kruskal‑Wallis"
        }
      }

      ## p‑value calculation
      fml <- as.formula(paste(var, "~", grp))
      p_val <- switch(test_name,
        "T‑test"        = t.test(fml, data = df_sub, var.equal = TRUE)$p.value,
        "Welch t"       = t.test(fml, data = df_sub, var.equal = FALSE)$p.value,
        "Mann‑Whitney U"= wilcox.test(fml, data = df_sub,
                                      exact = (prod(counts) <= 50))$p.value,
        "ANOVA"         = summary(aov(fml, data = df_sub))[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1],
        "Welch ANOVA"   = oneway.test(fml, data = df_sub, var.equal = FALSE)$p.value,
        "Kruskal‑Wallis"= kruskal.test(fml, data = df_sub)$p.value,
        NA_real_)

      out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
        Group                      = grp,
        Variable_Frequency_Display = freq_display,
        Sample_Size_Passed         = ss_pass,
        Variable                   = var,
        Comparison_Type            = paste(grp_type, "vs", detect_type(df_sub[[var]])),
        Normality                  = is_norm,
        Equal_Variance             = is_eq,
        Group_Difference_Test      = test_name,
        Group_Difference_P_Value   = round(p_val, 4),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
    }
  }
  out
}

# Example usage:
log_vars   <- paste0("log_", c("PM10_24hr","PM2.5_24hr","PM1_24hr","CO_24hr",
                               "PM10_c","PM2.5_c","PM1_c","BC_c","CO_c")) #this is just the log transformed varabiables
group_vars <- c("Type")

# Run:
combined_results <- combined_group_analysis(df, cont_vars = log_vars, group_vars = group_vars)

# Inspect:
combined_results

```

























Task1: Rerun the correlations between pollutant variables and FEVs/other health outcomes using the log-transformed variables (since normality is improved).
```{r}
correlation_continuous_only <- function(df, x_vars, y_vars, plots = FALSE) {
  if (!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) stop("Please install ggplot2")

  # Helper to flag extreme outliers (>1.5 * IQR)
  has_outlier <- function(x) {
    q <- stats::quantile(x, c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
    i <- diff(q)
    any(x < (q[1] - 1.5 * i) | x > (q[2] + 1.5 * i), na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  results <- data.frame(
    X_Var                   = character(),
    Y_Var                   = character(),
    N_Pairs                 = integer(),
    Normality_X             = logical(),
    Normality_Y             = logical(),
    Outlier_X               = logical(),
    Outlier_Y               = logical(),
    Correlation_Method      = character(),
    Linearity_Assumption    = character(),
    Monotonicity_Assumption = character(),
    Correlation_Value       = numeric(),
    P_Value                 = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors        = FALSE
  )

  for (x in x_vars) {
    for (y in y_vars) {
      xv <- df[[x]]
      yv <- df[[y]]
      keep <- complete.cases(xv, yv)
      n <- sum(keep)

      if (plots) {
        print(ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes_string(x = x)) +
                ggplot2::geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = .6) +
                ggplot2::labs(title = paste("Density of", x)) +
                ggplot2::theme_minimal())
        print(ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes_string(x = y)) +
                ggplot2::geom_density(fill = "salmon", alpha = .6) +
                ggplot2::labs(title = paste("Density of", y)) +
                ggplot2::theme_minimal())
      }

      if (n >= 3) {
        norm_x <- stats::shapiro.test(xv[keep])$p.value > 0.05
        norm_y <- stats::shapiro.test(yv[keep])$p.value > 0.05
        out_x <- has_outlier(xv[keep])
        out_y <- has_outlier(yv[keep])

        # Updated decision logic
        if (norm_x && norm_y && !out_x && !out_y) {
          method <- "Pearson"
          lin <- "TRUE"
          mono <- "Not Applicable"
        } else {
          method <- "Spearman"
          lin <- "Not Applicable"
          mono <- "TRUE"
        }

        ct <- cor.test(xv[keep], yv[keep], method = tolower(method))
        r <- unname(ct$estimate)
        p <- ct$p.value
      } else {
        norm_x <- norm_y <- out_x <- out_y <- NA
        method <- lin <- mono <- r <- p <- NA
      }

      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        X_Var = x,
        Y_Var = y,
        N_Pairs = n,
        Normality_X = norm_x,
        Normality_Y = norm_y,
        Outlier_X = out_x,
        Outlier_Y = out_y,
        Correlation_Method = method,
        Linearity_Assumption = lin,
        Monotonicity_Assumption = mono,
        Correlation_Value = round(r, 4),
        P_Value = round(p, 4),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }

  return(results)
}


# 1) Define your pollutant and lung‐function vectors
log_vars <- paste0("log_", c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
                             "PM10_c", "PM2.5_c", "PM1_c", "BC_c", "CO_c"))

lung_vars <- c(
  "FVC_Actual", "FVC_Predicted", "FVC_perc",
  "FEV1_Actual", "FEV1_Predicted", "FEV1_perc",
  "PEF_Actual", "PEF_Predicted", "PEF_perc"
)

# 2) Call the simplified continuous–continuous correlation function
corr_res <- correlation_continuous_only(
  df,
  x_vars = lung_vars,
  y_vars = log_vars,
  plots  = FALSE  # set TRUE if you want density/Q–Q plots
)

# 3) Inspect or export your results
print(corr_res)
write.csv(corr_res, file.path("results", "lung_vs_pollutant_corr.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```



















Health Outcome Variable:  we can't do the correlation when one of the health variable is categorical, we can only do continuous vs continuous, or binary vs continous, but we can do anova or test to test if groups are different, no need to do correlation

Step 1: For the ATS score value, there are NA value that is a category, so I need to create a subset of the dataframe to run the comparison test without NA response (violated minimum sample size assumption for category)

Step 1.1: Cough, Phlegm category comparison test without NA
```{r}
# Step 1: Define log-transformed variables
log_vars <- paste0("log_", c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
                             "PM10_c", "PM2.5_c", "PM1_c", "BC_c", "CO_c"))

# Step 2: Create a clean dataframe for each grouping variable separately

# For cough_category
df_cough_clean <- df[
  df$cough_category != "88" & 
  df$cough_category != "Missing" & 
  !is.na(df$cough_category), 
]
df_cough_clean$cough_category <- droplevels(df_cough_clean$cough_category)

# For phlegm_category
df_phlegm_clean <- df[
  df$phlegm_category != "88" & 
  df$phlegm_category != "Missing" & 
  !is.na(df$phlegm_category), 
]
df_phlegm_clean$phlegm_category <- droplevels(df_phlegm_clean$phlegm_category)

# For breathlessness_category
df_breathlessness_clean <- df[
  df$breathlessness_category != "88" & 
  df$breathlessness_category != "Missing" & 
  !is.na(df$breathlessness_category), 
]
df_breathlessness_clean$breathlessness_category <- droplevels(df_breathlessness_clean$breathlessness_category)

# For Wheezing
# Clean df_wheezing_clean by removing "88", "Missing", and NA values
df_wheezing_clean <- df[
  df$Wheezing != 88 & 
  df$Wheezing != "Missing" & 
  !is.na(df$Wheezing),
]



# Convert to factor and drop unused levels
df_wheezing_clean$Wheezing <- factor(df_wheezing_clean$Wheezing)
df_wheezing_clean$Wheezing <- droplevels(df_wheezing_clean$Wheezing)
levels(df_wheezing_clean$Wheezing) <- c("No wheeze", "Wheeze")
levels(df$Wheezing) <- c("No wheeze", "Wheeze")



# Step 3: Run analysis
res_cough     <- combined_group_analysis(df_cough_clean, cont_vars = log_vars, group_vars = "cough_category")
res_phlegm    <- combined_group_analysis(df_phlegm_clean, cont_vars = log_vars, group_vars = "phlegm_category")
res_breath    <- combined_group_analysis(df_breathlessness_clean, cont_vars = log_vars, group_vars = "breathlessness_category")
res_wheezing  <- combined_group_analysis(df_wheezing_clean, cont_vars = log_vars, group_vars = "Wheezing")

# Step 4: Combine and export
combined_results <- rbind(res_cough, res_phlegm, res_breath, res_wheezing)
write.csv(combined_results, file.path("results", "combined_group_analysis_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



```




Task 2: Redo the previous comparison for non-health outcome
2.1: From the previous code, combine/edit the variable
```{r}
#Village
focus_villages <- c("Mohuldiya", "Nelung", "Sankarpur","Jharbelda")
df$focus_group <- ifelse(df$Village %in% focus_villages, "Focus", "Regular")

#Education
# Reclassify education levels with combined categories
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Education_Grouped = factor(
    case_when(
      `Q1F Education` %in% c(0, 1)        ~ "No Formal Education",
      `Q1F Education` %in% c(2, 3)        ~ "Primary or Middle School",
      `Q1F Education` %in% c(4, 5, 6, 7)  ~ "High School and Above",
      TRUE                                ~ NA_character_
    ),
    levels = c(
      "No Formal Education",
      "Primary or Middle School",
      "High School and Above"
    )
  ))



#Electricity
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Electricity = case_when(
    `Q1P Electricity` == 2 ~ "No Electricity",  
    `Q1P Electricity` == 1 ~ "Has Electricity",  
    is.na(`Q1P Electricity`) ~ "Unknown/Missing",  # Handle missing values explicitly
    TRUE ~ "Other"  # Catch any unexpected values
  ))

# Convert to factor with ordered levels
df$Electricity <- factor(df$Electricity, 
                                 levels = c("No Electricity", "Has Electricity"))


#Income
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Income_Grouped = case_when(
    `Q1J avg fam income` == 1             ~ "Less than Rs 5000",
    `Q1J avg fam income` == 2             ~ "Rs 5000 – 10,000",
    `Q1J avg fam income` %in% c(3, 4)     ~ "More than Rs 10,000",
    TRUE                                  ~ NA_character_  # excludes 5 or NA
  )) %>%
  mutate(Income_Group = factor(Income_Grouped, levels = c(
    "Less than Rs 5000",
    "Rs 5000 – 10,000",
    "More than Rs 10,000"
  )))


#Roof Material
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Roof_Category = case_when(
      `Q4L roof material` %in% c(1, 3, 4) ~ "Non-Cement",  # Groups 1 (Khappar), 3 (Thatched), and 4 (Others)
      `Q4L roof material` == 2 ~ "Cement",                 # Group 2 (Cement tile),maybe wrost off check graphs
      TRUE ~ NA_character_                                  # Assigns NA if there are unexpected values
    )
  )

#Kitchen Ventilation
  # Create a new ventilation category variable
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Ventilation_Category = case_when(
    `Q4F open eves` == 2 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 2 ~ "No-No",
    `Q4F open eves` == 2 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 1 ~ "No-Yes",
    `Q4F open eves` == 1 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 2 ~ "Yes-No",
    `Q4F open eves` == 1 & `Q4G windows in the kitchen` == 1 ~ "Yes-Yes",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Handle missing values
  ))

  # Convert to an ordered factor for better comparisons
df$Kitchen_Ventilation_Category <- factor(df$Kitchen_Ventilation_Category, 
                                          levels = c("No-No", "No-Yes", "Yes-No", "Yes-Yes"))

#Smoke Exposure
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination = paste(
      `Q5A use incense`,
      `Q5B mosquito coils`,
      `Q5C keros lantern`,
      sep = "-"
    ),
    
    # Assign categories
    Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination = case_when(
      Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination %in% c("1-1-1", "1-1-2", "1-2-1", "2-1-1",
                               "1-1-3", "1-2-2", "2-1-2", "2-2-1") ~ "High Use",
      
      Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination %in% c("1-2-3", "1-3-1", "2-1-3", "2-2-2",
                               "2-3-1", "3-1-1", "3-1-2", "3-2-1", "3-2-2") ~ "Medium Use",
      
      TRUE ~ "Low Use"
    )
  )

#Indoor_Outdoor Kitchen Location
# Mapping values for Improved and Traditional cookstoves
df$indoor_outdoor_kitchen <- factor(df$`Q4A kitchen location`, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 6),
                                     labels = c('Indoor', 'Indoor', 'Indoor', 'Outdoor', 'Outdoor'))


# Household Size
df$HH_Size_Grouped <- case_when(
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "Small (1-3)",
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` %in% c(4, 5, 6) ~ "Medium (4-6)",
  df$`Q1O how many people live in HH` >= 7 ~ "Large (7+)",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Preserve NA values
)

df$HH_Size_Grouped <- factor(df$HH_Size_Grouped,
                             levels = c("Small (1-3)", "Medium (4-6)", "Large (7+)"))

# Wheezing HQ5A
df$Wheezing <- ifelse(df$HQ5A_apart == 1 | 
                                 df$HQ5A_cold == 1 | 
                                 df$HQ5A_most == 1, 1, 0)


#4/25/2025 New indoor and outdoor grouping (this is just testing)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Location_Category = case_when(
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 1             ~ "Kitchen in Living Area",
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 2             ~ "Separate Indoor Kitchen",
    `Q4A kitchen location` %in% c(3, 4)     ~ "Semi-Outdoor Kitchen",
    `Q4A kitchen location` == 6             ~ "Detached Outdoor Kitchen",
    TRUE                                    ~ NA_character_
  )) %>%
  mutate(Kitchen_Location_Category = factor(Kitchen_Location_Category, levels = c(
    "Kitchen in Living Area",
    "Separate Indoor Kitchen",
    "Semi-Outdoor Kitchen",
    "Detached Outdoor Kitchen"
  )))
```

Boxplot Function
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

boxplot_by_group <- function(df,
                             cont_var,
                             group_var) {
  d <- df
  d$.y    <- d[[cont_var]]
  # ← use the existing factor levels on the grouping column
  d$.grp  <- d[[group_var]]  
  d$.rown <- as.character(seq_len(nrow(d)))
  
  d0 <- subset(d, !is.na(.y) & !is.na(.grp))
  
  qs  <- quantile(d0$.y, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr <- diff(qs)
  out <- subset(d0, .y < qs[1] - 1.5*iqr | .y > qs[2] + 1.5*iqr)
  
  unit <- if (grepl("^CO", cont_var)) " (mg/m³)" else " (µg/m³)"
  y_lab <- paste0(cont_var, unit)
  
  p <- ggplot(d0, aes(x = .grp, y = .y, fill = .grp)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text(data = out,
              aes(label = .rown),
              nudge_x = 0.15, size = 3, color = "black") +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 12)) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Distribution of ", cont_var, " by ", group_var),
      x     = group_var,
      y     = y_lab
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 9, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  print(p)
}
```

HH_Size_Grouped
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Reorder levels first
df$HH_Size_Grouped <- factor(df$HH_Size_Grouped,
                             levels = c("Small (1-3)", "Medium (4-6)", "Large (7+)"))

# List of pollutant variables
polls <- c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
           "PM10_c",   "PM2.5_c",   "PM1_c",   "BC_c",   "CO_c")

# Loop through each pollutant and plot
for (v in polls) {
  df$y_var <- df[[v]]
  df$obs_id <- seq_len(nrow(df))  # for labeling outliers

  df_clean <- df[!is.na(df$y_var) & !is.na(df$HH_Size_Grouped), ]
  
  Qs <- quantile(df_clean$y_var, probs = c(0.25, 0.75))
  iqr <- diff(Qs)
  outliers <- df_clean[df_clean$y_var < Qs[1] - 1.5 * iqr | df_clean$y_var > Qs[2] + 1.5 * iqr, ]

  unit <- if (grepl("^CO", v)) " (mg/m³)" else " (µg/m³)"
  y_lab <- paste0(v, unit)

  p <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = HH_Size_Grouped, y = y_var, fill = HH_Size_Grouped)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text(data = outliers, aes(label = obs_id), nudge_x = 0.15, size = 3) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", v, "by Household Size Group"),
      x = "Household Size Group",
      y = y_lab
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  print(p)
}



# Loop through each log pollutant and plot
for (v in log_polls) {
  df$y_var <- df[[v]]
  df$obs_id <- seq_len(nrow(df))  # for labeling outliers

  df_clean <- df[!is.na(df$y_var) & !is.na(df$HH_Size_Grouped), ]
  
  Qs <- quantile(df_clean$y_var, probs = c(0.25, 0.75))
  iqr <- diff(Qs)
  outliers <- df_clean[df_clean$y_var < Qs[1] - 1.5 * iqr | df_clean$y_var > Qs[2] + 1.5 * iqr, ]

  unit <- if (grepl("^CO", v)) " (mg/m³)" else " (µg/m³)"
  y_lab <- paste0(v, unit)

  p <- ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = HH_Size_Grouped, y = y_var, fill = HH_Size_Grouped)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text(data = outliers, aes(label = obs_id), nudge_x = 0.15, size = 3) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", v, "by Household Size Group"),
      x = "Household Size Group",
      y = y_lab
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  print(p)
}
```

Descriptive Statistics
```{r}
descriptive_stats_by_group <- function(data, group_var, 
                                       variables = c("PM10_24hr", "PM2.5_24hr", "PM1_24hr", "CO_24hr",
                                                     "PM10_c",   "PM2.5_c",   "PM1_c",   "BC_c",   "CO_c"),
                                       log_prefix = "log_") {
  # Helper for summary stats
  get_descriptive_stats <- function(data, var_name) {
    x <- data[[var_name]]
    n        <- sum(!is.na(x))
    mean_val <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_val   <- sd(x,   na.rm = TRUE)
    se_val   <- sd_val / sqrt(n)
    min_val  <- min(x,  na.rm = TRUE)
    max_val  <- max(x,  na.rm = TRUE)
    ci_lower <- mean_val - qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_val
    ci_upper <- mean_val + qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_val

    data.frame(
      N           = n,
      Mean        = round(mean_val,  2),
      Std_Dev     = round(sd_val,    2),
      Std_Error   = round(se_val,    2),
      Lower_Bound = round(ci_lower,  2),
      Upper_Bound = round(ci_upper,  2),
      Minimum     = round(min_val,   2),
      Maximum     = round(max_val,   2),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }

  # Ensure grouping var is factor
  data[[group_var]] <- as.factor(data[[group_var]])

  # Build a function to do one set of variables
  build_table <- function(var_list, suffix) {
    stats_tbl <- do.call(rbind, lapply(var_list, function(var) {
      do.call(rbind, lapply(levels(data[[group_var]]), function(lev) {
        subset_df <- data[data[[group_var]] == lev, ]
        stats     <- get_descriptive_stats(subset_df, var)
        stats$Variable <- var
        stats$Group    <- lev
        stats
      }))
    }))
    # reorder columns
    stats_tbl <- stats_tbl[, c("Variable", "Group", "N", "Mean", "Std_Dev", 
                               "Std_Error", "Lower_Bound", "Upper_Bound", 
                               "Minimum", "Maximum")]
    # replace negatives
    num_cols <- setdiff(names(stats_tbl), c("Variable","Group"))
    stats_tbl[num_cols] <- lapply(stats_tbl[num_cols], function(col) {
      col[col < 0] <- NA
      col
    })
    # write out
    desktop_path <- "results"
    out_file     <- file.path(desktop_path, paste0(group_var, "_", suffix, "_desc_stats.csv"))
    write.csv(stats_tbl, out_file, row.names = FALSE)
    message("Saved ", suffix, " stats to: ", out_file)
    stats_tbl
  }

  # 1) Regular variables
  regular_tbl <- build_table(variables, "regular")
  print(regular_tbl)

  # 2) Log-transformed variables
  log_vars <- paste0(log_prefix, variables)
  # check that those columns exist
  missing_logs <- setdiff(log_vars, names(data))
  if (length(missing_logs) > 0) {
    warning("These log-variables not found in data and will be skipped:\n  ", 
            paste(missing_logs, collapse = ", "))
    log_vars <- setdiff(log_vars, missing_logs)
  }
  if (length(log_vars) > 0) {
    log_tbl <- build_table(log_vars, "log")
    print(log_tbl)
  } else {
    message("No log variables to process.")
  }

  invisible(list(regular = regular_tbl, log = if (exists("log_tbl")) log_tbl else NULL))
}
```

```{r}
# 3. Boxplots for raw pollutant variables by stove type

}

# 4. Boxplots for log-transformed pollutant variables by stove type

}


# 5. For statistical difference

                               "PM10_c","PM2.5_c","PM1_c","BC_c","CO_c")) #this is just the log transformed varabiables

# Run:
```

2."Group Comparison: ""Using the variables Type (Improved vs Traditional) and focus_group (Focus vs Regular), conduct pairwise subgroup comparisons between the following combinations:
Improved–Focus vs Improved–Regular

```{r}
)
```

Univeriate Model Comparison Part
Step 1: Convert the response variable into factor 
Step 2: fit univeriate model with Type vs other binary/categorical varaible
        * profile likelihood CIs are generally preferred over Wald CIs for logistic regression coefficients because they tend to have better coverage properties, especially if the sample size isn't very large or if the likelihood function isn't perfectly symmetric. They are derived by inverting the likelihood ratio test.
        * Wald Test: Yes. The summary(model_focus) output directly provides the Wald test statistics (z-values) and their p-values for each coefficient (i.e., for each level of focus_group compared to the reference).


Type Univeriate Regression
```{r}
# 0) Make sure your outcome is a 2-level factor
df$Type <- factor(df$Type, levels = c("Traditional", "Improved"))

# 1) focus_group
df$focus_group <- relevel(factor(df$focus_group), ref = "Regular")
model_focus <- glm(Type ~ focus_group, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_focus)
exp(coef(model_focus))        # Odds ratios
exp(confint(model_focus))
drop1(model_focus, test = "LRT")  # Likelihood‐ratio test

# 2) Education_Grouped
model_edu <- glm(Type ~ Education_Grouped, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_edu)
exp(coef(model_edu))    # Odds ratios
exp(confint(model_edu)) 
drop1(model_edu, test = "LRT")  # Likelihood‐ratio test




# 3) Electricity
model_elec <- glm(Type ~ Electricity, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_elec)
exp(coef(model_elec))
exp(confint(model_elec))
drop1(model_elec, test = "LRT")



# 4) Income_Grouped
df$Income_Grouped <- factor(df$Income_Grouped, 
                            levels = c("Less than Rs 5000", 
                                       "Rs 5000 – 10,000", 
                                       "More than Rs 10,000"),
                            ordered = FALSE)
model_income <- glm(Type ~ Income_Grouped, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_income)
exp(coef(model_income))
exp(confint(model_income))
drop1(model_income, test = "LRT")



# 5) Roof_Category
df$Roof_Category <- relevel(factor(df$Roof_Category), ref = "Non-Cement")
model_roof <- glm(Type ~ Roof_Category, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_roof)
exp(coef(model_roof))
exp(confint(model_roof))
drop1(model_roof, test = "LRT")


# 6) Kitchen_Ventilation_Category
model_vent <- glm(Type ~ Kitchen_Ventilation_Category, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_vent)
exp(coef(model_vent))
exp(confint(model_vent))
drop1(model_vent, test = "LRT")



# 7) Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination
df$Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination <- relevel(factor(df$Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination), ref = "Low Use")
model_smoke <- glm(Type ~ Smoke_Exposure_Usage_Combination, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_smoke)
exp(coef(model_smoke))
exp(confint(model_smoke))
drop1(model_smoke, test = "LRT")


# 8) indoor_outdoor_kitchen
model_io <- glm(Type ~ indoor_outdoor_kitchen, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_io)
exp(coef(model_io))
exp(confint(model_io))
drop1(model_io, test = "LRT")


# 9) HH_Size_Grouped
model_hh <- glm(Type ~ HH_Size_Grouped, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_hh)
exp(coef(model_hh))
exp(confint(model_hh))
drop1(model_hh, test = "LRT")





# 10) cough_category
model_cough <- glm(as.factor(Type) ~ cough_category, data = df_cough_clean, family = binomial)
summary(model_cough)
exp(coef(model_cough))        # Odds ratios
exp(confint(model_cough))     # 95% CI for ORs
drop1(model_cough, test = "LRT")  # Global LRT for cough_category

# 11) phlegm_category
model_phlegm <- glm(as.factor(Type) ~ phlegm_category, data = df_phlegm_clean, family = binomial)
summary(model_phlegm)
exp(coef(model_phlegm))
exp(confint(model_phlegm))
drop1(model_phlegm, test = "LRT")


# 12) breathlessness_category
model_breath <- glm(as.factor(Type) ~ breathlessness_category, data = df_breathlessness_clean, family = binomial)
summary(model_breath)
exp(coef(model_breath))
exp(confint(model_breath))
drop1(model_breath, test = "LRT")

# 13) Wheezing
model_wheezing <- glm(as.factor(Type) ~ Wheezing, data = df_wheezing_clean, family = binomial)
summary(model_wheezing)
exp(coef(model_wheezing))
exp(confint(model_wheezing))
drop1(model_wheezing, test = "LRT")



# 14) Kitchen_Location_Category
model_kloc <- glm(Type ~ Kitchen_Location_Category, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(model_kloc)
exp(coef(model_kloc))
exp(confint(model_kloc)) #odds ratio confidence interval
drop1(model_kloc, test = "LRT")



```


Type Univeriate Regression
```{r}
# 1) log_PM10_24hr
model_type_pm10 <- lm(log_PM10_24hr ~ Type, data = df)
summary(model_type_pm10)
```

Wheezing Univeriate Regression
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# Univariate Logistic Regressions Predicting Wheezing (Yes vs No)
# Using df_sub so you only subset once
# ============================================================================

# 0) Ensure Wheezing is a 2‐level factor with the correct labels
if (!all(levels(df$Wheezing) == c("No wheeze", "Wheeze"))) {
  df$Wheezing <- factor(
    df$Wheezing,
    levels = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("No wheeze", "Wheeze")
  )
}

# 0b) Subset once to non-missing Wheezing and drop unused factor levels
df_sub <- droplevels(df[!is.na(df$Wheezing), ])

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) focus_group → Wheezing ~ focus_group
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
model_wz_focus <- glm(Wheezing ~ focus_group,
                      data   = df_sub,
                      family = binomial)
summary(model_wz_focus)
exp(coef(model_wz_focus))
exp(confint(model_wz_focus))
drop1(model_wz_focus, test = "LRT")

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Education_Grouped → Wheezing ~ Education_Grouped

                    data   = df_sub,

exp(coef(model_wz_edu))
exp(confint(model_wz_edu))
drop1(model_wz_edu, test = "LRT")

exp(confint(model_wz_elec))
drop1(model_wz_elec, test = "LRT")

# 4) Income_Grouped → Wheezing ~ Income_Grouped
```

FEV Univeriate Regression
Create a new ratio variable using the FEVs actual / FEVs predicted, then fit a unvieriate linear regression for each log pollutant variable
1.FVC_ratio
```{R}
# 1. Calculate FVC_ratio
df$FVC_ratio <- 100 * df$FVC_Actual / df$FVC_Predicted

# 2. Clean dataframe
df_fvc_ratio <- df[!is.na(df$FVC_ratio), ]

# 3. Univariate regressions (FVC_ratio)
model_fvc_ratio_pm10 <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_PM10_24hr, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_pm10)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm10)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm10, test = "F")

model_fvc_ratio_pm25 <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_PM2.5_24hr, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_pm25)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm25)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm25, test = "F")

confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm1)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm1, test = "F")

model_fvc_ratio_co <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_CO_24hr, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_co)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_co)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_co, test = "F")

model_fvc_ratio_pm10c <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_PM10_c, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_pm10c)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm10c)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm10c, test = "F")

model_fvc_ratio_pm25c <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_PM2.5_c, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_pm25c)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm25c)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm25c, test = "F")

model_fvc_ratio_pm1c <- lm(FVC_ratio ~ log_PM1_c, data = df_fvc_ratio)
summary(model_fvc_ratio_pm1c)
confint(model_fvc_ratio_pm1c)
drop1(model_fvc_ratio_pm1c, test = "F")

drop1(model_fvc_ratio_coc, test = "F")
```

FVC_ratio_bin
```{r}
# 1. Create binary variable
df$FVC_ratio_bin <- as.integer(df$FVC_ratio < 80 & !is.na(df$FVC_ratio))
df_fvc_ratio_bin <- df[!is.na(df$FVC_ratio_bin), ]

# 2. Univariate logistic regressions with drop1 LRT
model_fvcbin_pm10   <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM10_24hr,  data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm10);   confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm10);   drop1(model_fvcbin_pm10, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_pm25   <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM2.5_24hr, data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm25);   confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm25);   drop1(model_fvcbin_pm25, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_pm1    <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM1_24hr,   data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm1);    confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm1);    drop1(model_fvcbin_pm1, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_co     <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_CO_24hr,    data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_co);     confint.default(model_fvcbin_co);     drop1(model_fvcbin_co, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_pm10c  <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM10_c,     data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm10c);  confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm10c);  drop1(model_fvcbin_pm10c, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_pm25c  <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM2.5_c,    data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm25c);  confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm25c);  drop1(model_fvcbin_pm25c, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_pm1c   <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_PM1_c,      data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_pm1c);   confint.default(model_fvcbin_pm1c);   drop1(model_fvcbin_pm1c, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_bc     <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_BC_c,       data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_bc);     confint.default(model_fvcbin_bc);     drop1(model_fvcbin_bc, test = "LRT")

model_fvcbin_coc    <- glm(FVC_ratio_bin ~ log_CO_c,       data = df_fvc_ratio_bin, family = binomial)
summary(model_fvcbin_coc);    confint.default(model_fvcbin_coc);    drop1(model_fvcbin_coc, test = "LRT")


```

FVC_ratio_bin (3 level)
```{r}
df$FVC_ratio_ord <- cut(
  df$FVC_ratio,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 49, 79, Inf),
  labels = c("Severe impairment (<50%)", "Moderate impairment (50-79%)", "Normal (≥80%)"),
  right = TRUE,
  ordered_result = TRUE
)


# Use only non-missing FVC_ratio_ord
df_fvc_ratio_ord <- df[!is.na(df$FVC_ratio_ord), ]



run_ord_reg <- function(df, outcome_var, predictor_var) {
  # 1. Subset to complete cases
  vars <- c(outcome_var, predictor_var)
  df2  <- df[complete.cases(df[vars]), , drop = FALSE]
  
  # 2. Fit the proportional-odds model
  f    <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", predictor_var))
  mod  <- polr(f, data = df2, Hess = TRUE)
  
  # 3. Extract Wald statistics
  s    <- summary(mod)
  ct   <- coef(s)
  tval <- ct[predictor_var, "t value"]
  pval <- 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(tval)))
  
  # 4. 95% CI on log-odds (approximate)
  ci_log <- confint.default(mod)             #  matrix with rows for each coef & intercept
  ci_p   <- ci_log[predictor_var, ]          #  numeric vector length 2: c(lower, upper)
  
  # 5. Odds ratio and its CI
  beta   <- coef(mod)[predictor_var]
  or     <- exp(beta)
  ci_or  <- exp(ci_p)
  
  # 6. Build result data.frame
  res <- data.frame(
    Predictor    = predictor_var,
    Beta         = beta,
    SE           = ct[predictor_var, "Std. Error"],
    `t value`    = tval,
    `p value`    = pval,
    CI_log_lower = ci_p[1],
    CI_log_upper = ci_p[2],
    OR           = or,
    CI_OR_lower  = ci_or[1],
    CI_OR_upper  = ci_or[2],
    row.names    = NULL,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(res)
}

run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM10_24hr")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM2.5_24hr")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM1_24hr")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_CO_24hr")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM10_c")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM2.5_c")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_PM1_c")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_BC_c")
run_ord_reg(df, "FVC_ratio_ord", "log_CO_c")

```
