Model Selection (Outcome = Wheezing)

Method 1: Backward Selection (Pollutant + Non-health variable)
```{r}
# Required Packages & Libraries (ensure these are installed and loaded)
# ============================
# if (!requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE))        install.packages("car")

# ================================================
# Function: One-step Backward Selection by LRT (with auto-detection for quasi-family)
# ================================================
backward_selection_LRT <- function(
  df,
  outcome,
  predictors,
  locked_predictors = NULL, # New argument: a character vector of predictor names to always keep
  p_cut = 0.20,
  family = binomial # Default starting family
) {
  suppressPackageStartupMessages(require(car)) # For drop1 test="F"

  # 1) Validate and separate predictors into locked and selectable
  # Ensure locked_predictors are also in the df and initial predictors
  if (!is.null(locked_predictors)) {
    locked_predictors <- locked_predictors[nzchar(locked_predictors) & locked_predictors %in% names(df)]
    # Ensure locked predictors are part of the initial predictors list
    if (any(!locked_predictors %in% predictors)) {
      message("Warning: Some specified locked_predictors were not in the initial 'predictors' list. Adding them.")
      predictors <- unique(c(predictors, locked_predictors))
    }
  } else {
    locked_predictors <- character() # Ensure it's an empty character vector if NULL
  }

  initial_all_predictors <- predictors[nzchar(predictors) & predictors %in% names(df)]
  selectable_predictors_initial <- setdiff(initial_all_predictors, locked_predictors)

  if (length(initial_all_predictors) == 0 && length(locked_predictors) == 0) {
      stop("No valid predictors (locked or selectable) provided or found in data frame.")
  }

  # --- Auto-detection of appropriate family based on initial full model dispersion ---

  cat("--- Auto-detecting optimal GLM family for selection ---\n")

  # Fit an initial full model with the provided 'family' (usually binomial)
  initial_full_form <- if (length(initial_all_predictors) == 0) {
    as.formula(paste(outcome, "~ 1"))
  } else {
    as.formula(paste(outcome, "~", paste(initial_all_predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  }

  initial_full_fit <- tryCatch(
    suppressWarnings(glm(initial_full_form, data = df, family = family)),
    error = function(e) {
      stop(paste("Error fitting initial full model for family detection:", e$message))
    }
  )

  if (is.null(initial_full_fit) || !initial_full_fit$converged || length(coef(initial_full_fit)) == 0) {
    stop("Initial full model failed to converge or has no estimable coefficients. Cannot proceed with selection.")
  }

  # Calculate dispersion parameter
  initial_dispersion_param <- initial_full_fit$deviance / initial_full_fit$df.residual
  cat(sprintf("Initial full model dispersion parameter (using %s family): %.3f\n",
              deparse(substitute(family)), initial_dispersion_param))

  selected_family <- family # Start with the provided family

  # Check for over/underdispersion and suggest quasibinomial/quasipoisson
  # Only suggest if the initial family was binomial or poisson
  if (deparse(substitute(family)) %in% c("binomial", "poisson")) {
    if (initial_dispersion_param > 1.5 || initial_dispersion_param < 0.8) { # Using common thresholds
      if (deparse(substitute(family)) == "binomial") {
        selected_family <- quasibinomial()
        cat(sprintf("  Detected significant dispersion (%.3f). Switching to `family = quasibinomial()` for selection.\n", initial_dispersion_param))
      } else if (deparse(substitute(family)) == "poisson") {
        selected_family <- quasipoisson()
        cat(sprintf("  Detected significant dispersion (%.3f). Switching to `family = quasipoisson()` for selection.\n", initial_dispersion_param))
      }
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("  Dispersion (%.3f) is within acceptable range. Continuing with `family = %s()`.\n",
                  initial_dispersion_param, deparse(substitute(family))))
    }
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("  Non-standard family (`%s`) provided. Dispersion check for auto-detection skipped. Proceeding with provided family.\n",
                deparse(substitute(family))))
  }

  # --- End of Auto-detection ---

  # Now, proceed with backward selection using the 'selected_family'

  current_selectable_predictors <- selectable_predictors_initial
  dropped_terms <- character()

  # Determine if the selected family is a quasi-family for drop1 test
  is_selected_family_quasi <- inherits(selected_family, "quasibinomial") ||
                              inherits(selected_family, "quasipoisson") ||
                              (is.function(selected_family) &&
                                 (deparse(substitute(selected_family)) == "quasibinomial" ||
                                  deparse(substitute(selected_family)) == "quasipoisson"))

  repeat {
    # Combine locked and current selectable predictors for the current model
    current_all_predictors <- c(locked_predictors, current_selectable_predictors)

    # 2) Build the current formula
    if (length(current_all_predictors) == 0) {
      form <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~ 1"))
    } else {
      form <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~", paste(current_all_predictors, collapse = " + ")))
    }

    # 3) Fit the model
    fit <- tryCatch(
      suppressWarnings(glm(form, data = df, family = selected_family)),
      error = function(e) {
        message("Warning: Model failed to fit during selection step with family='", deparse(substitute(selected_family)), "': ", e$message, ". Stopping backward selection.")
        return(NULL)
      }
    )

    if (is.null(fit) || !fit$converged || length(coef(fit)) == 0) {
      message("Warning: Model failed to converge or has no estimable coefficients during selection step. Stopping backward selection.")
      break
    }

    # If only locked predictors or no selectable predictors left, stop
    if (length(current_selectable_predictors) == 0) {
        cat("No selectable predictors left to drop.\n")
        break
    }

    # 4) Compute drop1 LRT table (excluding locked terms and intercept)
    test_type <- if (is_selected_family_quasi) "F" else "Chisq"
    pvals_col_name <- if (is_selected_family_quasi) "Pr(>F)" else "Pr(>Chi)"

    # Use drop1, then filter to only consider selectable predictors
    d1_full <- tryCatch(
      drop1(fit, test = test_type), # Do not remove intercept yet
      error = function(e) {
        message("Error in drop1() with test='", test_type, "': ", e$message)
        return(NULL)
      }
    )

    if (is.null(d1_full) || nrow(d1_full) <= 1) { # Check if only intercept or no terms left
        if (length(current_selectable_predictors) > 0) {
            message("Warning: drop1() output is empty or only contains intercept, but selectable predictors remain. Stopping.")
        }
        break
    }

    # Filter out intercept and locked predictors from drop1 output
    d1 <- d1_full[rownames(d1_full) %in% current_selectable_predictors, , drop = FALSE]

    if (nrow(d1) == 0) { # If no selectable terms left in d1 after filtering, stop
        cat("No selectable predictors with drop1 information remaining.\n")
        break
    }

    pvals <- d1[[pvals_col_name]]
    if (is.null(pvals)) {
      message("Warning: P-value column '", pvals_col_name, "' not found in filtered drop1 output. Stopping backward selection.")
      break
    }
    names(pvals) <- rownames(d1)

    # 5) Stop if all selectable p-values are less than or equal to cutoff
    if (all(is.na(pvals)) || max(pvals, na.rm = TRUE) <= p_cut) {
      if (length(pvals) > 0 && max(pvals, na.rm = TRUE) == -Inf) {
          message("Warning: All p-values for selectable terms were NA. Stopping backward selection.")
      }
      break
    }

    # 6) Otherwise, drop the selectable term with the largest p-value
    worst_term <- names(which.max(pvals))
    worst_p    <- pvals[worst_term]

    cat(sprintf("Dropping: %-20s (LRT p = %.3f)\n", worst_term, worst_p))
    dropped_terms <- c(dropped_terms, worst_term)
    current_selectable_predictors <- setdiff(current_selectable_predictors, worst_term)
  }

  # 7) Report dropped terms & final model refit
  cat("\nTerms dropped (LRT p >", p_cut, "):\n")
  if (length(dropped_terms) == 0) {
    cat("  None\n")
  } else {
    print(dropped_terms)
  }
  cat("\nTerms locked and always kept:\n")
  if (length(locked_predictors) == 0) {
    cat("  None\n")
  } else {
    print(locked_predictors)
  }


  final_all_predictors <- c(locked_predictors, current_selectable_predictors)

  if (length(final_all_predictors) == 0) {
    final_formula <- paste(outcome, "~ 1")
  } else {
    final_formula <- paste(outcome, "~", paste(final_all_predictors, collapse = " + "))
  }
  cat("\nFinal model formula (using selected family:", deparse(substitute(selected_family)), "):\n ", final_formula, "\n\n")

  # Final fit of the selected model using the 'selected_family'
  final_fit <- glm(as.formula(final_formula), data = df, family = selected_family)

  return(list(
    model = final_fit,
    dropped = dropped_terms,
    locked = locked_predictors, # Also return locked terms
    formula = final_formula,
    final_family_used = selected_family # Return the actual family object used
  ))

}
# =======================
# === Example Usage: ====
# =======================

# 1) Define your candidate predictors
# 1) Define your predictors
log_vars <- paste0("log_", c("PM10_24hr","PM2.5_24hr","PM1_24hr","CO_24hr",
                             "PM10_c","PM2.5_c","PM1_c","BC_c","CO_c"))
group_vars <- c(
  "Type", "focus_group", "Education_Grouped", "Electricity",
  "Income_Grouped", "Roof_Category",
  "Kitchen_Ventilation_Usage_Combination", "HH_Size_Grouped",
  "Kitchen_Location_Category"
)

lung_vars <- c(
  "FVC_Actual", "FVC_Predicted", "FVC_perc",
  "FEV1_Actual", "FEV1_Predicted", "FEV1_perc",
  "PEF_Actual", "PEF_Predicted", "PEF_perc"
)

all_predictors <- c(group_vars,group_vars)  # Combine both
other_vars = c(
  "Education_Grouped", 
  "Income_Grouped", "Roof_Category",
  "Kitchen_Ventilation_Usage_Combination", "HH_Size_Grouped"
)

locked_vars <- c(
  "Type",
  #log_vars[2:2],                    # "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr"
  "Electricity",
  "focus_group",
  "indoor_outdoor_kitchen"
)

# 2) Run one-step backward LRT selection
# 2) Run one-step backward LRT selection
res_LRT_locked <- backward_selection_LRT(
  df                = df,
  outcome           = "Wheezing",
  predictors        = other_vars,
  locked_predictors = locked_vars,
  p_cut             = 0.20,
  family            = binomial
)

# 3) Inspect what was dropped
cat("\nDropped terms:\n")
print(res_LRT_locked$dropped)

# 4) View the final model summary
cat("\n=== Final Model Summary ===\n")
print(summary(res_LRT_locked$model))

# 5) (Optional) Extract ORs and 95% CIs
or  <- exp(coef(res_LRT_locked$model))
ci  <- exp(confint.default(res_LRT_locked$model))
results <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(or),
  OR       = or,
  CI_lower = ci[,1],
  CI_upper = ci[,2]
)
cat("\n=== Final Model Odds Ratios & 95% CIs ===\n")
print(results, row.names = FALSE)



```


Wheezing 24hr level mediation analysis
```{R}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Set up
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")
all_vars <- c("Wheezing", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc <- df[complete.cases(df[, all_vars]), ]

n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "Wheezing") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_24hr", "PM10_24hr", "PM1_24hr"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```




Using PCA to combine 24hr level then assess the indirect of mediator
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Make the composite (PC1) for 24hr pollutants
poll_mat  <- scale(df_cc[, c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")])
pca_res   <- prcomp(poll_mat)
df_cc$PM_PC1_24hr <- pca_res$x[, 1]

# 2. Fit the mediator model
m_pc1 <- lm(
  PM_PC1_24hr ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc
)

# 3. Fit the outcome model (use glm for compatibility with mediation)
y_pc1 <- glm(
  Wheezing ~ Type + PM_PC1_24hr + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Run mediate() with parallel bootstrapping if available
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_pc1,
  model.y       = y_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "PM_PC1_24hr",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 5. Summarize and table results
sum_pc1 <- summary(med_out_pc1)
print(sum_pc1)

results_pca_pc1 <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1$d0, sum_pc1$z0, sum_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1$d0.p, sum_pc1$z0.p, sum_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1)



```


Wheezing pollutant cooking medidation analysis
```{R}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Setup
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM10_c", "log_PM1_c")
all_vars <- c("Wheezing", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc <- df[complete.cases(df[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "Wheezing") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_c", "PM10_c", "PM1_c"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)



```


Using PCA to combine cooking level then assess the indirect of mediator
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Create composite PC1 from concurrent pollutant levels
poll_mat_c  <- scale(df_cc[, c("log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM10_c", "log_PM1_c")])
pca_res_c   <- prcomp(poll_mat_c)
df_cc$PM_PC1_c <- pca_res_c$x[, 1]

# 2. Fit mediator model
m_pc1_c <- lm(
  PM_PC1_c ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc
)

# 3. Fit outcome model (use glm for mediation compatibility)
y_pc1_c <- glm(
  Wheezing ~ Type + PM_PC1_c + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Mediation analysis with parallel bootstrapping (if supported)
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_pc1_c <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_pc1_c,
  model.y       = y_pc1_c,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "PM_PC1_c",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 5. Summarize and create result table
sum_pc1_c <- summary(med_out_pc1_c)
print(sum_pc1_c)

results_pca_pc1_c <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0, sum_pc1_c$z0, sum_pc1_c$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1_c$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1_c$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1_c$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1_c$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.p, sum_pc1_c$z0.p, sum_pc1_c$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1_c)


```



Wheezing pollutant FEV medidation analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Detect available cores, leave one for system
n_cores <- detectCores() - 1

# Define confounders and exposure
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"

# Subset to complete cases for required variables
all_vars <- c("Wheezing", exposure,
              "FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin",
              confounders)
df_cc <- df[complete.cases(df[, all_vars]), ]

# Unified function for a single mediator
boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "Wheezing") {
  # 1. ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # 2. ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # 3. Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  # Bootstrap with parallel computing
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  # 95% CIs
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  # Means
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
med_names <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
results_list <- lapply(med_names, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator   = c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin"),
  ACME       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total      = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```




```{r}
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Prepare Data (Make sure variables exist and are complete)
lung_bin_vars <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars <- c("Wheezing", "Type", lung_bin_vars, confounders)
df_cc <- df_cc[complete.cases(df_cc[, all_vars]), ]

# 2. PCA on binary lung function indicators
lung_bin_mat <- scale(df_cc[, lung_bin_vars])
pca_res_lung <- prcomp(lung_bin_mat)
df_cc$Lung_PC1 <- pca_res_lung$x[, 1]

# 3. Mediator Model (linear model for Lung_PC1)
m_lung_pc1 <- lm(
  Lung_PC1 ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc
)

# 4. Outcome Model (logistic regression for Wheezing)
y_lung_pc1 <- glm(
  Wheezing ~ Type + Lung_PC1 + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc, family = binomial
)

# 5. Mediation Analysis with parallel computing
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_lung_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_lung_pc1,
  model.y       = y_lung_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "Lung_PC1",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 6. Summarize and table results
sum_lung_pc1 <- summary(med_out_lung_pc1)
print(sum_lung_pc1)

results_pca_lung <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0, sum_lung_pc1$z0, sum_lung_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_lung_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_lung_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_lung_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_lung_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.p, sum_lung_pc1$z0.p, sum_lung_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_lung)



```














Method 1: Backward Selection (Pollutant + Non-health variable)										
													
```{r}
check_logistic_assumptions <- function(model,
                                       vif_cut   = 10,
                                       resid_cut = 3,
                                       corr_threshold = 0.999,
                                       seed      = NULL) {
  # Load necessary packages. Using `suppressPackageStartupMessages`
  # to avoid cluttering the console with messages when packages load.
  suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    require(car)
    require(ResourceSelection)
    require(ggplot2)
    require(dplyr) # For better data manipulation, especially with plot data
    require(purrr) # For map_df in linearity plots
  })

  # Set seed for reproducibility if provided
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)

  # --- Start of New Collinearity Pre-Check ---
  initial_model <- model # Keep a copy of the original model
  
  if (!inherits(initial_model, "glm")) {
      stop("Input 'model' must be a glm object.")
  }

  original_data <- model.frame(initial_model) # Get the data used for the model
  initial_formula <- formula(initial_model)
  outcome_var <- all.vars(initial_formula)[1]
  
  # Get the family from the initial model, needed for re-fitting
  model_family <- family(initial_model) # Extract the family object

  vars_to_drop_perfect_corr <- character()

  if (length(names(original_data)[sapply(original_data, is.numeric) & names(original_data) != outcome_var]) >= 2) {
    numeric_predictors <- names(original_data)[sapply(original_data, is.numeric) & names(original_data) != outcome_var]
    
    # Calculate correlation matrix only for numeric predictors
    cor_matrix <- cor(original_data[, numeric_predictors, drop = FALSE], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

    # Find pairs with correlation >= corr_threshold (absolute value)
    highly_correlated_pairs <- which(abs(cor_matrix) >= corr_threshold & upper.tri(cor_matrix), arr.ind = TRUE)

    if (nrow(highly_correlated_pairs) > 0) {
      cat("\n==== Pre-check: Handling Near-Perfect Collinearity ====\n")
      cat("  Identified variables with correlation >= ", corr_threshold, "\n")
      for (i in 1:nrow(highly_correlated_pairs)) {
        var1 <- rownames(cor_matrix)[highly_correlated_pairs[i, 1]]
        var2 <- colnames(cor_matrix)[highly_correlated_pairs[i, 2]]

        # Arbitrarily choose to remove the second variable found in the pair
        # Ensure it hasn't already been marked for removal by another pair
        if (!(var2 %in% vars_to_drop_perfect_corr)) {
          vars_to_drop_perfect_corr <- c(vars_to_drop_perfect_corr, var2)
          message(sprintf("  Removing '%s' due to near-perfect correlation (%.3f) with '%s'.", var2, cor_matrix[var1, var2], var1))
        }
      }
      
      if (length(vars_to_drop_perfect_corr) > 0) {
          # Update the model formula by removing the identified variables
          current_terms <- attr(terms(initial_formula), "term.labels")
          
          # Only keep terms that are not in vars_to_drop_perfect_corr
          keep_terms <- setdiff(current_terms, vars_to_drop_perfect_corr)
          
          if (length(keep_terms) == 0) {
              stop("All predictor variables removed due to perfect collinearity. No model can be fit.")
          }
          
          new_formula <- reformulate(keep_terms, response = outcome_var)
          
          # Re-fit the model with the reduced formula and original data
          # This is critical to get a stable model for diagnostics
          model <- tryCatch({
              update(initial_model, new_formula, data = original_data, family = model_family) # Pass family explicitly
          }, error = function(e) {
              stop(paste("Failed to refit model after removing perfectly correlated variables:", e$message))
          })
          
          cat("  Model refitted after handling perfect collinearity. Diagnostics will proceed with this updated model.\n")
      } else {
          cat("  No perfectly correlated numeric predictors found.\n")
      }
    } else {
      cat("  No perfectly correlated numeric predictors found.\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("  Not enough numeric predictors for collinearity check (need at least 2).\n")
  }
  # --- End of New Collinearity Pre-Check ---


  # Helper function to run diagnostics on a given model
  run_diagnostics <- function(m, label) {
    if (is.null(m)) {
      cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Model is NULL. No diagnostics.\n", label))
      return()
    }
    
    # Ensure the model frame is accessible
    dfm       <- tryCatch(model.frame(m), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(dfm)) {
      cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Could not extract model frame. No diagnostics.\n", label))
      return()
    }

    outcome <- all.vars(formula(m))[1]
    # Handle cases where outcome might not be directly found in dfm (e.g., if it's a calculated column)
    if (!outcome %in% names(dfm)) {
      # Attempt to get outcome from the original data if available
      # This part relies on the original_data being defined in the outer scope
      if (exists("original_data") && outcome %in% names(original_data)) {
        dfm[[outcome]] <- original_data[[outcome]]
      } else {
        cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Outcome variable '%s' not found in model frame or original data. Some diagnostics may fail.\n", label, outcome))
      }
    }

    probs     <- predict(m, type = "response")
    # Add a small epsilon to avoid log(0) for logit calculation
    logit     <- log(pmax(probs, 1e-8) / pmax(1 - probs, 1e-8))
    vd        <- residuals(m, type = "deviance")
    cooks     <- cooks.distance(m)
    lev       <- hatvalues(m)
    n         <- nrow(dfm)
    p         <- length(coef(m)) # Number of coefficients, including intercept
    
    # Identify continuous variables more robustly, excluding the outcome
    # and ensuring they are indeed numeric and not factors that might be misidentified.
    cont      <- names(dfm)[sapply(dfm, is.numeric) & names(dfm) != outcome]

    cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Model Summary ====\n", label))
    print(summary(m))

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: VIF (Multicollinearity) ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: VIF > 5-10 indicates problematic collinearity.\n")
    vif_raw <- tryCatch(car::vif(m), error = function(e) {
      cat("  Error calculating VIF:", e$message, "\n")
      NULL
    })
    if (is.null(vif_raw)) {
      cat("  VIF not available.\n")
    } else {
      print(vif_raw)
      if (any(vif_raw > vif_cut, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        cat(sprintf("  WARNING: Some VIF values are above the cutoff of %g. Consider removing variables.\n", vif_cut))
      } else {
        cat("  All VIF values are below the cutoff. Multicollinearity is likely not an issue.\n")
      }
    }

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Box–Tidwell Linearity ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: A p-value < 0.05 indicates a significant non-linear component.\n")
    if (length(cont) == 0) {
      cat("  No continuous variables found for linearity check.\n")
    } else {
      for (v in cont) {
        # Create a temporary dataframe for Box-Tidwell to avoid modifying the original dfm
        temp_df_bt <- dfm
        # Ensure the variable has positive values for log transformation
        if (any(temp_df_bt[[v]] <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
          cat(sprintf("  %s: skipped (Contains non-positive values, cannot take log for Box-Tidwell).\n", v))
          next # Skip to next variable
        }
        temp_df_bt[[paste0(v, "_log")]] <- temp_df_bt[[v]] * log(temp_df_bt[[v]])
        
        # Ensure outcome variable exists in temp_df_bt for the formula
        if (!outcome %in% names(temp_df_bt)) {
            temp_df_bt[[outcome]] <- dfm[[outcome]] # Add if it somehow got lost
        }

        bt <- tryCatch(
          glm(as.formula(paste0(outcome, " ~ ", v, " + ", v, "_log")),
              data = temp_df_bt, family = binomial()),
          error = function(e) e
        )
        if (inherits(bt, "error")) {
          cat(sprintf("  %s: failed (Error: %s)\n", v, bt$message))
        } else if (nrow(summary(bt)$coefficients) < 3) {
           cat(sprintf("  %s: failed (Not enough coefficients for Box-Tidwell test. Check for multicollinearity or data issues in the sub-model.)\n", v))
        }
        else {
          # Check if the log term is in the coefficients table
          log_term_name <- paste0(v, "_log")
          if (log_term_name %in% rownames(summary(bt)$coefficients)) {
              pval <- summary(bt)$coefficients[log_term_name, "Pr(>|z|)"]
              cat(sprintf("  %s: p=%.3f [%s]\n",
                          v, pval,
                          ifelse(pval > 0.05, "OK", "VIOLATION")))
              if (pval <= 0.05) {
                cat("    Consider transformations (e.g., polynomial, log) for this variable.\n")
              }
          } else {
              cat(sprintf("  %s: skipped (Box-Tidwell log term '%s' not found in sub-model summary. Check for issues in sub-model fit).\n", v, log_term_name))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Influence & Outliers ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: Identify observations that unduly affect model estimates.\n")
    cat(sprintf("  Cook's D >4/n: %d points (first few: %s)\n",
                sum(cooks > 4/n),
                paste(head(which(cooks > 4/n), 10), collapse = ", ")))
    if (sum(cooks > 4/n) > 0) cat("    Consider investigating these points for data errors or genuine influential nature.\n")
    cat(sprintf("  |Dev resid| >%g: %d points (first few: %s)\n",
                resid_cut,
                sum(abs(vd) > resid_cut),
                paste(head(which(abs(vd) > resid_cut), 10), collapse = ", ")))
    if (sum(abs(vd) > resid_cut) > 0) cat(sprintf("    These are potential outliers in the response. Investigate their fit.\n"))
    cat(sprintf("  Leverage >2p/n: %d points (cutoff=%.3f)\n",
                sum(lev > 2*p/n), 2*p/n))
    if (sum(lev > 2*p/n) > 0) cat("    These points have unusual predictor values. Examine if they are valid.\n")

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Separation ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: Perfect separation occurs when a predictor perfectly predicts the outcome.\n")
    if (any(abs(coef(m)) > 10, na.rm = TRUE) || any(is.infinite(coef(m)))) {
      cat("  WARNING: possible separation (some coefficients are very large or infinite).\n")
      cat("    This can lead to unstable estimates and inflated standard errors.\n")
      cat("    Consider adding more data, removing problematic predictors, or using Firth's bias correction.\n")
    } else {
      cat("  OK: No obvious signs of perfect separation.\n")
    }

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Hosmer–Lemeshow Goodness of Fit ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: A high p-value (typically > 0.05) indicates good model fit.\n")
    # Ensure outcome is numeric 0/1 for Hosmer-Lemeshow
    outcome_numeric <- as.numeric(as.factor(dfm[[outcome]])) - 1 # Converts to 0/1 for factors

    hl <- tryCatch(
      ResourceSelection::hoslem.test(outcome_numeric, probs, g = 10),
      error = function(e) {
        cat("  Error calculating Hosmer-Lemeshow test:", e$message, "\n")
        NULL
      }
    )
    if (!is.null(hl)) {
      print(hl)
      if (hl$p.value < 0.05) {
        cat("  WARNING: Hosmer-Lemeshow test suggests poor model fit.\n")
        cat("    Consider re-evaluating predictors or model structure.\n")
      } else {
        cat("  Hosmer-Lemeshow test suggests good model fit.\n")
      }
    }

    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Overdispersion/Underdispersion ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: For binomial GLMs, dispersion parameter should be close to 1.\n")
    cat("  Values > 1.5-2 suggest overdispersion (inflated SEs, lower p-values).\n")
    cat("  Values < 1 (e.g., 0.5) suggest underdispersion (deflated SEs, higher p-values).\n")
    
    # Calculate dispersion parameter (Residual Deviance / Residual Df)
    dispersion_param <- m$deviance / m$df.residual
    cat(sprintf("  Calculated Dispersion Parameter: %.3f\n", dispersion_param))
    if (dispersion_param > 1.5) {
      cat("  WARNING: Potential overdispersion detected.\n")
      cat("    Consider using `family = quasibinomial` or a mixed-effects model if data are clustered.\n")
    } else if (dispersion_param < 0.8) {
      cat("  WARNING: Potential underdispersion detected.\n")
      cat("    This is less common for binomial models, but may indicate model misspecification.\n")
    } else {
      cat("  Dispersion parameter is within an acceptable range (close to 1).\n")
    }


    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Diagnostic Plots ----\n", label))
    # Cook's D plot
    print(
      ggplot(data.frame(obs = 1:n, cooks = cooks),
             aes(obs, cooks)) +
        geom_segment(aes(xend = obs, yend = 0), colour = "grey60") +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 4/n, linetype = 2, colour = "red") +
        labs(title = paste(label, "Cook's D (Influence)"), x = "Observation Index", y = "Cook's D") +
        theme_minimal()
    )
    # Deviance Residuals plot
    print(
      ggplot(data.frame(obs = 1:n, vd = vd),
             aes(obs, vd)) +
        geom_segment(aes(xend = obs, yend = 0), colour = "grey60") +
        geom_hline(yintercept = c(-resid_cut, resid_cut),
                   linetype = 2, colour = "red") +
        labs(title = paste(label, "Deviance Residuals (Outliers)"),
             x = "Observation Index", y = "Deviance Residual") +
        theme_minimal()
    )
    # Leverage plot
    print(
      ggplot(data.frame(obs = 1:n, lev = lev),
             aes(obs, lev)) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 2*p/n, linetype = 2, colour = "red") +
        labs(title = paste(label, "Leverage (Unusual Predictor Values)"),
             x = "Observation Index", y = "Leverage") +
        theme_minimal()
    )
    
    # NEW: Residuals vs. Predicted Plot
    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Residuals vs. Predicted Plot ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: Look for patterns (e.g., funnel shape, curves) which suggest issues.\n")
    cat("  Ideally, residuals should be scattered randomly around zero.\n")
    
    # For binomial, deviance residuals are often preferred.
    # The plot looks for patterns in deviance residuals against predicted log-odds (linear predictor)
    # or predicted probabilities. Using linear predictor often reveals patterns better.
    linear_predictor <- predict(m, type = "link")
    print(
      ggplot(data.frame(linear_predictor = linear_predictor, dev_residuals = vd),
             aes(x = linear_predictor, y = dev_residuals)) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
        labs(title = paste(label, "Deviance Residuals vs. Linear Predictor"),
             x = "Linear Predictor (Log-odds)", y = "Deviance Residual") +
        theme_minimal()
    )

    # Linearity Plots
    cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Linearity Plots ----\n", label))
    cat("  Interpretation: The LOESS line (blue) should be approximately linear.\n")
    if (length(cont) == 0) {
      cat("  No continuous variables for linearity plots.\n")
    } else {
      dfm$logit <- logit # Add logit to the dataframe for plotting
      for (v in cont) {
        print(
          ggplot(dfm, aes_string(x = v, y = "logit")) +
            geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
            geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, colour = "blue") +
            labs(title = paste(label, "Linearity for", v),
                 x = v, y = "Logit (Smoothed)") +
            theme_minimal()
        )
      }
    }
  }

  # 1) Run diagnostics on the model (potentially updated by pre-check)
  run_diagnostics(model, "Before Pruning")

  # 2) VIF–pruning (drop one at random when multiple exceed)
  pruned  <- model
  dropped <- character()
  
  # Ensure the model is a glm object (already checked, but good for robustness)
  if (!inherits(pruned, "glm")) {
    cat("\nInput 'model' is not a glm object after pre-checking. VIF pruning skipped.\n")
    return(invisible(list(before = initial_model, after = NULL, dropped = NULL)))
  }

  repeat {
    vs_raw <- tryCatch(car::vif(pruned), error = function(e) {
      cat("  Error during VIF calculation in pruning: ", e$message, "\n")
      NULL
    })

    if (is.null(vs_raw)) {
      cat("  VIF calculation failed or no more terms to check for VIF. Stopping VIF pruning.\n")
      break
    }

    # Extract the effective VIF for each term:
    if (is.matrix(vs_raw)) {
      eff_vif <- vs_raw[, "GVIF^(1/(2*Df))"]
    } else {
      eff_vif <- vs_raw
    }
    
    # Exclude intercept if present in eff_vif
    if ("(Intercept)" %in% names(eff_vif)) {
      eff_vif <- eff_vif[names(eff_vif) != "(Intercept)"]
    }
    
    # Find all terms > cutoff
    high_terms <- names(eff_vif)[eff_vif > vif_cut]
    if (length(high_terms) == 0) break

    # Randomly pick one to drop
    to_drop <- sample(high_terms, 1)
    dropped <- c(dropped, to_drop)
    cat(sprintf("\nDropping for high VIF: %s  (VIF=%.2f)\n",
                to_drop, eff_vif[to_drop]))

    # Update the model
    resp  <- all.vars(formula(pruned))[1]
    # Get current terms, filtering out the one to drop
    current_terms <- attr(terms(pruned), "term.labels")
    keep  <- setdiff(current_terms, to_drop)
    
    if (length(keep) == 0) {
      cat("  All predictor terms dropped; only intercept would remain. Stopping VIF pruning.\n")
      pruned <- NULL # Indicate no meaningful model left
      break
    }
    
    newf  <- reformulate(keep, response = resp)
    pruned <- tryCatch(update(pruned, newf, data = original_data, family = model_family), error = function(e) { # Pass family explicitly
      cat("  Model update failed during VIF pruning:", e$message, "; stopping.\n")
      NULL
    })
    
    if (is.null(pruned)) {
      cat("  Model update resulted in a NULL model; stopping VIF pruning.\n")
      break
    }
  }

  if (length(dropped) > 0) {
    cat("\nTerms dropped for VIF >", vif_cut, " (excluding initial perfect collinearity drops):\n")
    print(dropped)
  }

  # 3) Final diagnostics
  # Check if pruned is still a valid model and has more than just an intercept
  if (!is.null(pruned) && length(coef(pruned)) > 1) {
    run_diagnostics(pruned, "After Pruning")
  } else {
    cat("\nPruned model is NULL or only intercept remains. No 'After Pruning' diagnostics.\n")
  }

  # Return original model (before any pruning) and the final pruned model
  invisible(list(before = initial_model, after = pruned, dropped = dropped))
}



```


```{r}
# --- HELPER FUNCTION TO RUN DIAGNOSTICS ---
run_diagnostics <- function(m, label) {
  if (is.null(m) || !inherits(m, "glm")) {
    cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Model is not a valid GLM object. Skipping diagnostics. ====\n", label))
    return()
  }
  
  # Ensure model frame is available for metrics
  dfm <- model.frame(m)
  if (nrow(dfm) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Model frame is empty. Skipping diagnostics. ====\n", label))
    return()
  }

  # Calculate metrics
  n <- nrow(dfm)
  p <- length(coef(m))
  cooks <- cooks.distance(m)
  lev <- hatvalues(m)
  dev_resid <- residuals(m, type = "deviance")
  std_resid <- rstandard(m, type = "deviance")

  # Print summaries
  cat(sprintf("\n==== %s: Model Summary ====\n", label))
  print(summary(m))

  cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: VIF (Multicollinearity) ----\n", label))
  if (p > 2) { # VIF requires at least 2 predictors
    try(print(car::vif(m)), silent = TRUE)
  } else {
    cat("  NOTE: VIF check requires at least 2 predictors.\n")
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Goodness of Fit (Hosmer-Lemeshow) ----\n", label))
  # H-L test is only for binomial, not quasibinomial
  if(family(m)$family == "binomial") {
      try(print(ResourceSelection::hoslem.test(dfm[[1]], fitted(m), g = 10)), silent = TRUE)
  } else {
      cat("  NOTE: Hosmer-Lemeshow test is not applicable for quasi-families.\n")
  }

  # --- Diagnostic Plots ---
  cat(sprintf("\n---- %s: Diagnostic Plots ----\n", label))
  
  # Plot 1: Residuals vs. Fitted Plot (Checks for linearity of the logit)
  print(ggplot(data.frame(fitted = predict(m, type="link"), resid = dev_resid), aes(fitted, resid)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
      labs(title = paste(label, "Residuals vs. Fitted"), x = "Linear Predictor (Logit)", y = "Deviance Residuals") + theme_minimal())

  # Plot 2: Cook's Distance
  print(ggplot(data.frame(obs = 1:n, cooks = cooks), aes(obs, cooks)) +
      geom_bar(stat="identity") + geom_hline(yintercept = 4/n, linetype = 2, colour = "red") +
      labs(title = paste(label, "Cook's Distance"), x = "Observation Index") + theme_minimal())
  
  # Plot 3: Leverage vs. Standardized Residuals
  print(ggplot(data.frame(lev = lev, resid = std_resid), aes(lev, resid)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 2*p/n, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") +
      labs(title = paste(label, "Leverage vs. Standardized Residuals"), x = "Leverage", y = "Standardized Residuals") + theme_minimal())
}


# --- MAIN FUNCTION ---
check_logistic_assumptions <- function(model,
                                       vif_cut = 5,
                                       resid_cut = 3,
                                       seed = NULL) {
  # --- Load necessary packages ---
  suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    require(car)
    require(ResourceSelection)
    require(ggplot2)
    require(dplyr)
  })

  # --- Initial Setup ---
  if (!inherits(model, "glm")) {
    stop("Input 'model' must be a valid glm object.")
  }
  
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  
  initial_model <- model

  # --- Step 1: Diagnostics for the original model ---
  cat("========================================================\n")
  cat("===== Step 1: Diagnostics on Your Original Model =====\n")
  cat("========================================================\n")
  run_diagnostics(initial_model, "Original Model")

  # --- Step 2: Identify and remove influential points ---
  cat("\n\n========================================================\n")
  cat("===== Step 2: Removing Influential Outliers ==========\n")
  cat("========================================================\n")
  
  original_data <- model.frame(initial_model)
  n <- nrow(original_data)
  p <- length(coef(initial_model))
  cooks <- cooks.distance(initial_model)
  lev <- hatvalues(initial_model)
  dev_resid <- residuals(initial_model, type = "deviance")
  
  influential_indices <- which(cooks > (4/n) | lev > (2*p/n) | abs(dev_resid) > resid_cut)
  
  if (length(influential_indices) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("Found %d influential/outlier points to remove.\n", length(influential_indices)))
    data_for_refit <- original_data[-influential_indices, ]
    
    # Attempt to refit the model; handle errors gracefully
    model_after_removal <- tryCatch({
      update(initial_model, data = data_for_refit)
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("\n*** WARNING: Model failed to refit after removing influential points. ***\n")
      cat("This often happens if removal causes perfect separation. VIF pruning will proceed on the original model.\n")
      cat("Reason:", e$message, "\n")
      return(initial_model) # IMPORTANT: If refit fails, return the original model
    })
    
  } else {
    cat("No influential points found to remove based on the specified cutoffs.\n")
    model_after_removal <- initial_model
  }

  # --- Step 3: Iteratively remove variables with high VIF ---
  cat("\n\n========================================================\n")
  cat("===== Step 3: Pruning for Multicollinearity (VIF) ====\n")
  cat("========================================================\n")

  final_model <- model_after_removal
  
  repeat {
    # Stop if there are fewer than 2 predictors
    if (length(coef(final_model)) <= 2) {
        cat("Fewer than 2 predictors remain. Stopping VIF pruning.\n")
        break
    }

    vif_values <- tryCatch(car::vif(final_model), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(vif_values)) break

    # Handle both matrix (for factors) and vector VIF output
    effective_vif <- if(is.matrix(vif_values)) vif_values[, "GVIF^(1/(2*Df))"]^2 else vif_values
    max_vif <- max(effective_vif, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if (max_vif <= vif_cut) {
        cat("All VIF values are below the cutoff of", vif_cut, ". No variables pruned.\n")
        break
    }
    
    var_to_drop <- names(which.max(effective_vif))
    cat(sprintf("Pruning: Dropping '%s' (VIF = %.2f)\n", var_to_drop, max_vif))
    
    new_formula <- update(formula(final_model), as.formula(paste(". ~ . -", var_to_drop)))
    final_model <- update(final_model, formula = new_formula)
  }
  
  # --- Step 4: Final diagnostics ---
  cat("\n\n========================================================\n")
  cat("===== Step 4: Diagnostics on the Final Cleaned Model =====\n")
  cat("========================================================\n")
  run_diagnostics(final_model, "Final Cleaned Model")
  
  invisible(list(initial_model = initial_model, final_model = final_model))
}

check_logistic_assumptions(model = res_LRT_locked$model) 
```















Model 2: Cough	
```{r}
# 1. Keep only real categories (drop “Missing”)
df_cc2 <- df[df$cough_category != "Missing", ]

# 2. Make a binary cough variable
df_cc2$cough_binary <- factor(
  ifelse(df_cc2$cough_category == "No Cough",
         "No Cough",
         "Any Cough"),
  levels = c("No Cough", "Any Cough")
)

library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")
all_vars <- c("cough_binary", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc <- df_cc2[complete.cases(df_cc2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "cough_binary") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_24hr", "PM10_24hr", "PM1_24hr"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Cough Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```



Using PCA for cough through combine of 24hr level
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Build the PCA-based mediator PC1 from 24-hour pollutant levels
poll_mat  <- scale(df_cc[, c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")])
pca_res   <- prcomp(poll_mat)
df_cc$PM_PC1 <- pca_res$x[, 1]

# 2. Mediator model: PC1 ~ cookstove type + confounders
m_pc1 <- lm(
  PM_PC1 ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc
)

# 3. Outcome model: Use glm for mediation compatibility (logistf output can't be used in mediate())
y_pc1 <- glm(
  cough_binary ~ Type + PM_PC1 + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Causal mediation analysis with parallel bootstrapping if supported
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_pc1,
  model.y       = y_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "PM_PC1",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 5. Summarize mediation results
sum_pc1 <- summary(med_out_pc1)
print(sum_pc1)

results_pca_pc1 <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1$d0, sum_pc1$z0, sum_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1$d0.p, sum_pc1$z0.p, sum_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1)


```


Mediation of cookstove → Cough by pollutant cooking
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM10_c", "log_PM1_c")
all_vars <- c("cough_binary", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc <- df_cc2[complete.cases(df_cc2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "cough_binary") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_c", "PM10_c", "PM1_c"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Cough Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```

```{r}
  data = df_cc
)

  data = df_cc,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Mediation analysis with parallel bootstrapping (if supported)
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

  model.m       = m_pc1_c,

  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0, sum_pc1_c$z0, sum_pc1_c$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1_c$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1_c$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1_c$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1_c$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1_c$d0.p, sum_pc1_c$z0.p, sum_pc1_c$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1_c)
```

Cough Mediation analysis through FEV
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Make sure cough_binary exists! If not, create it first as shown earlier:
# df_cc2 <- df[df$cough_category != "Missing", ]
# df_cc2$cough_binary <- factor(
#   ifelse(df_cc2$cough_category == "No Cough", "No Cough", "Any Cough"),
#   levels = c("No Cough", "Any Cough")
# )

n_cores <- detectCores() - 1

confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"

# Subset to complete cases for required variables
all_vars <- c("cough_binary", exposure,
              "FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin",
              confounders)
df_cc <- df_cc2[complete.cases(df_cc2[, all_vars]), ]

# Unified function for a single mediator
boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "cough_binary") {
  # 1. ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # 2. ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # 3. Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  # Bootstrap with parallel computing
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  # 95% CIs
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  # Means
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
med_names <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
results_list <- lapply(med_names, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator   = c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin"),
  ACME       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total      = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```


Cough Mediation analysis through FEV PCA
```{r}
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Prepare Data (make sure all variables exist and are complete)
lung_bin_vars <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
confounders   <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars      <- c("cough_binary", "Type", lung_bin_vars, confounders)
df_cc <- df_cc2[complete.cases(df_cc2[, all_vars]), ]

# 2. PCA on binary lung function indicators
lung_bin_mat <- scale(df_cc[, lung_bin_vars])
pca_res_lung <- prcomp(lung_bin_mat)
df_cc$Lung_PC1 <- pca_res_lung$x[, 1]

# 3. Mediator Model (linear regression for Lung_PC1)
m_lung_pc1 <- lm(
  Lung_PC1 ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc
)

# 4. Outcome Model (logistic regression for cough_binary)
y_lung_pc1 <- glm(
  cough_binary ~ Type + Lung_PC1 + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc, family = binomial
)

# 5. Mediation Analysis with parallel computing
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_lung_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_lung_pc1,
  model.y       = y_lung_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "Lung_PC1",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 6. Summarize and table results
sum_lung_pc1 <- summary(med_out_lung_pc1)
print(sum_lung_pc1)

results_pca_lung <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0, sum_lung_pc1$z0, sum_lung_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_lung_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_lung_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_lung_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_lung_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_lung_pc1$d0.p, sum_lung_pc1$z0.p, sum_lung_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_lung)


```







Cough
```{r}
# 1. Define your predictors and locked variables for cough
all_predictors <- c("Type", "Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen",
                    "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")
locked_vars     <- c("Type", "Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")

# 2. Run backward selection on the cough outcome
res_LRT_locked_cough <- backward_selection_LRT(
  df                = df_cc,            # use the complete‐cases cough data frame
  outcome           = "cough_binary",     # new binary outcome
  predictors        = other_vars,
  locked_predictors = locked_vars,
  p_cut             = 0.20,
  family            = binomial
)

# 3. Inspect which terms were dropped
cat("\nDropped terms:\n")
print(res_LRT_locked_cough$dropped)

# 4. View the final model summary
cat("\n=== Final Cough Model Summary ===\n")
print(summary(res_LRT_locked_cough$model))

# 5. Extract Odds Ratios & 95% CIs for the final model
or_cough <- exp(coef(res_LRT_locked_cough$model))
ci_cough <- exp(confint.default(res_LRT_locked_cough$model))
results_cough <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(or_cough),
  OR       = or_cough,
  CI_lower = ci_cough[,1],
  CI_upper = ci_cough[,2]
)
cat("\n=== Final Cough Model Odds Ratios & 95% CIs ===\n")
print(results_cough, row.names = FALSE)

```


									
```{r}
check_logistic_assumptions(res_LRT_locked_cough$model)
```



Model3: phlegm
Mediation of cookstove → phlegm by 24 hr pollutant levels
```{r}
# 1. Keep only real categories (drop “Missing”)
df2 <- df[df$phlegm_category != "Missing", ]

# 2. Make a binary phlegm variable
df2$phlegm_binary <- factor(
  ifelse(df2$phlegm_category == "No Phlegm",
         "No Phlegm",
         "Any Phlegm"),
  levels = c("No Phlegm", "Any Phlegm")
)

library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Define locked confounders and exposure
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")
all_vars <- c("phlegm_binary", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "phlegm_binary") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_24hr", "PM10_24hr", "PM1_24hr"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Phlegm Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```


Using PCA to combine all three 24hr level to assess the indirect effect of the all 24hr level as a mediator
```{R}
library(logistf)
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Build the PCA-based mediator PC1 from 24hr pollutant levels
poll_mat  <- scale(df_cc_phlegm[, c("log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr")])
pca_res   <- prcomp(poll_mat)
df_cc_phlegm$PM_PC1 <- pca_res$x[, 1]

# 2. Mediator model: PC1 ~ cookstove type + confounders
m_pc1 <- lm(
  PM_PC1 ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc_phlegm
)

# 3. Outcome model: Use glm for compatibility with mediate()
y_pc1 <- glm(
  phlegm_binary ~ Type + PM_PC1 + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc_phlegm,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Causal mediation analysis with parallel support
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_pc1,
  model.y       = y_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "PM_PC1",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 5. Summarize mediation results as a table
sum_pc1 <- summary(med_out_pc1)
print(sum_pc1)

results_pca_pc1 <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1$d0, sum_pc1$z0, sum_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1$d0.p, sum_pc1$z0.p, sum_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1)



```



Mediation of cookstove → phlegm by pollutant cooking
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Define confounders and exposure
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
mediators   <- c("log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM10_c", "log_PM1_c")
all_vars <- c("phlegm_binary", exposure, mediators, confounders)
df_cc_phlegm <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = "phlegm_binary") {
  # ACME (Indirect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))), data = d)
    )["TypeImproved"]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (Direct)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  # Total Effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~ Type +", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc_phlegm, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc_phlegm, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc_phlegm, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run for each mediator
results_list <- lapply(mediators, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = c("PM2.5_c", "PM10_c", "PM1_c"),
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Phlegm Mediation Results (ACME, ADE, Total Effect and 95% CI)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```



Using PCA to combine  cooking level to assess the indirect effect of the all cooking level as a mediator
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(mediation)
library(parallel)

# 1. Build the PCA-based mediator PC1 from concurrent (_c) pollutant levels
poll_mat  <- scale(df_cc_phlegm[, c("log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM10_c", "log_PM1_c")])
pca_res   <- prcomp(poll_mat)
df_cc_phlegm$PM_PC1 <- pca_res$x[, 1]

# 2. Mediator model: Linear regression of PC1
m_pc1 <- lm(
  PM_PC1 ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc_phlegm
)

# 3. Outcome model: Use glm for compatibility with mediate()
y_pc1 <- glm(
  phlegm_binary ~ Type + PM_PC1 + Electricity + focus_group + indoor_outdoor_kitchen,
  data = df_cc_phlegm,
  family = binomial
)

# 4. Causal mediation analysis with parallel support
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
med_out_pc1 <- mediate(
  model.m       = m_pc1,
  model.y       = y_pc1,
  treat         = "Type",
  mediator      = "PM_PC1",
  control.value = "Traditional",
  treat.value   = "Improved",
  boot          = TRUE,
  sims          = 5000,
  parallel      = "multicore"
)

# 5. Summarize mediation results as a table
sum_pc1 <- summary(med_out_pc1)
print(sum_pc1)

results_pca_pc1 <- data.frame(
  Effect      = c("ACME (Indirect)", "ADE (Direct)", "Total Effect"),
  Estimate    = c(sum_pc1$d0, sum_pc1$z0, sum_pc1$tau.coef),
  CI_Lower    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[1], sum_pc1$z0.ci[1], sum_pc1$tau.ci[1]),
  CI_Upper    = c(sum_pc1$d0.ci[2], sum_pc1$z0.ci[2], sum_pc1$tau.ci[2]),
  p.value     = c(sum_pc1$d0.p, sum_pc1$z0.p, sum_pc1$tau.p)
)
print(results_pca_pc1)
```
