FEV to Cookstove to Wheeze Mediation Analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Define variables
fev_vars     <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
mediator     <- "Type"
outcome      <- "Wheezing"
confounders  <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars     <- c(outcome, mediator, fev_vars, confounders)

# Subset to complete cases
df_cc <- df[complete.cases(df[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Mediation analysis function
boot_mediation_effects_fev <- function(exposure_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect via Cookstove Type)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_em <- coef(
      glm(as.formula(paste(mediator, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
          data = d, family = binomial)
    )[exposure_name]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_me_y <- fit_y$coefficients[paste0(mediator, "Improved")] # Adjust if coding differs
    beta_em * beta_me_y
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  # Total effect (no mediator)
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation for all three exposures
results_list_fev <- lapply(fev_vars, boot_mediation_effects_fev)

results_df_fev <- data.frame(
  Exposure    = fev_vars,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (Exposure = FEV/FVC/PEF Ratio, Mediator = Cookstove, Outcome = Wheezing)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df_fev,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```


FEV to Cookstove to Cough Mediation Analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Keep only real categories (drop “Missing”)
df_cc2 <- df[df$cough_category != "Missing", ]

# 2. Make a binary cough variable
df_cc2$cough_binary <- factor(
  ifelse(df_cc2$cough_category == "No Cough",
         "No Cough",
         "Any Cough"),
  levels = c("No Cough", "Any Cough")
)

# Define exposures and confounders
fev_vars     <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
mediator     <- "Type"
outcome      <- "cough_binary"
confounders  <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars     <- unique(c(outcome, mediator, fev_vars, confounders))

# Subset to complete cases
df_cc <- df_cc2[complete.cases(df_cc2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Mediation analysis function for FEV/FVC/PEF exposures
boot_mediation_effects_fev <- function(exposure_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect via Cookstove Type)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_em <- coef(
      glm(as.formula(paste(mediator, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
          data = d, family = binomial)
    )[exposure_name]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_me_y <- fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"] # Adjust if needed
    beta_em * beta_me_y
  }
  # ADE (direct effect of FEV variable)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  # Total effect (no mediator)
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation analysis for each FEV/FVC/PEF variable
results_list_fev <- lapply(fev_vars, boot_mediation_effects_fev)

results_df_fev <- data.frame(
  Exposure    = fev_vars,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (Exposure = FEV/FVC/PEF Ratio, Mediator = Cookstove, Outcome = Cough)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df_fev,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```



FEV to Cookstove to Phlegm Mediation Analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Keep only real categories (drop “Missing”)
df2 <- df[df$phlegm_category != "Missing", ]

# 2. Make a binary phlegm variable
df2$phlegm_binary <- factor(
  ifelse(df2$phlegm_category == "No Phlegm",
         "No Phlegm",
         "Any Phlegm"),
  levels = c("No Phlegm", "Any Phlegm")
)

# Set up variables
fev_vars    <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
mediator    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "phlegm_binary"
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars    <- unique(c(outcome, mediator, fev_vars, confounders))

# Subset to complete cases
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Mediation analysis function for FEV/FVC/PEF exposures
boot_mediation_effects_fev <- function(exposure_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect via Cookstove Type)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_em <- coef(
      glm(as.formula(paste(mediator, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
          data = d, family = binomial)
    )[exposure_name]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_me_y <- fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"] # Adjust if needed
    beta_em * beta_me_y
  }
  # ADE (direct effect of FEV variable)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  # Total effect (no mediator)
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation analysis for each FEV/FVC/PEF variable
results_list_fev <- lapply(fev_vars, boot_mediation_effects_fev)

results_df_fev <- data.frame(
  Exposure    = fev_vars,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (Exposure = FEV/FVC/PEF Ratio, Mediator = Cookstove, Outcome = Phlegm)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df_fev,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```


FEV to Cookstove to Breathlessness
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# 1. Drop “Missing” in breathlessness_category
df2 <- df[df$breathlessness_category != "Missing", ]

# 2. Create a two‐level factor: No vs. Any breathlessness
df2$breathless_binary <- factor(
  ifelse(df2$breathlessness_category == "No Breathlessness",
         "No Breathlessness",
         "Any Breathlessness"),
  levels = c("No Breathlessness", "Any Breathlessness")
)

# Set up variables
fev_vars    <- c("FVC_ratio_bin", "FEV1_ratio_bin", "PEF_ratio_bin")
mediator    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "breathless_binary"
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
all_vars    <- unique(c(outcome, mediator, fev_vars, confounders))

# Subset to complete cases
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Mediation analysis function for FEV/FVC/PEF exposures
boot_mediation_effects_fev <- function(exposure_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect via Cookstove Type)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_em <- coef(
      glm(as.formula(paste(mediator, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
          data = d, family = binomial)
    )[exposure_name]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_me_y <- fit_y$coefficients["TypeImproved"]
    beta_em * beta_me_y
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", mediator, "+", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  # Total effect (no mediator)
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = " + "))),
      data = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[exposure_name]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation analysis for each FEV/FVC/PEF variable
results_list_fev <- lapply(fev_vars, boot_mediation_effects_fev)

results_df_fev <- data.frame(
  Exposure    = fev_vars,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list_fev, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Mediation Results (Exposure = FEV/FVC/PEF Ratio, Mediator = Cookstove, Outcome = Breathlessness)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df_fev,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```



Create new variable lung disease using all three FEV ratio (combine the income level for testing at this step)
```{r}
#table(df$FEV1_ratio_bin)
#table(df$FVC_ratio_bin)
#table(df$PEF_ratio_bin)

df$lung_disease <- ifelse(
  df$FEV1_ratio_bin == 0 | df$FVC_ratio_bin == 0 | df$PEF_ratio_bin == 0,
  1, 0
) # 10% vs 90%
prop.table(table(df$lung_disease))
locked_conf <- c("Type", "Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
other_vars  <- c("Education_Grouped", "Roof_Category",
                 "HH_Size_Grouped")
res_LRT_locked_lung_disease <- backward_selection_LRT(
  df                = df,            # use the complete‐cases cough data frame
  outcome           = "lung_disease",     # new binary outcome
  predictors        = other_vars,
  locked_predictors = locked_vars,
  p_cut             = 0.20,
  family            = binomial
)

# 3) Inspect what was dropped
cat("\nDropped terms:\n")
print(res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$dropped)

# 4) View the final model summary
cat("\n=== Final Model Summary ===\n")
print(summary(res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$model))



```










Cookstove (exposure) to Pollutant (mediator) to FVC_ratio_bin (outcome) mediation analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Detect available cores, leave one for system
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Define variables
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "FVC_ratio_bin"
pollutants  <- c(
  "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr", "log_CO_24hr",
  "log_PM10_c", "log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM1_c", "log_BC_c", "log_CO_c"
)

# Subset to complete cases for all used variables
all_vars <- c(outcome, exposure, pollutants, confounders)
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]

# Mediation function for one mediator
boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))), data = d)
    )[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  # Total effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation analysis for each pollutant
results_list <- lapply(pollutants, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = pollutants,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### FVC_ratio_bin Mediation Results (Pollutants as Mediator)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```


Cookstove (exposure) to Pollutant (mediator) to FEV1_ratio_bin (outcome) mediation analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

# Detect available cores, leave one for system
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

# Define variables
confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "FEV1_ratio_bin"   # <--- Updated outcome here!
pollutants  <- c(
  "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr", "log_CO_24hr",
  "log_PM10_c", "log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM1_c", "log_BC_c", "log_CO_c"
)

# Subset to complete cases for all used variables
all_vars <- c(outcome, exposure, pollutants, confounders)
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]

# Mediation function for one mediator
boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))), data = d)
    )[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  # Total effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

# Run mediation analysis for each pollutant
results_list <- lapply(pollutants, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = pollutants,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### FEV1_ratio_bin Mediation Results (Pollutants as Mediator)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```



Cookstove (exposure) to Pollutant (mediator) to PEF_ratio_bin (outcome) mediation analysis
```{R}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "PEF_ratio_bin"   # <--- Only change is here
pollutants  <- c(
  "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr", "log_CO_24hr",
  "log_PM10_c", "log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM1_c", "log_BC_c", "log_CO_c"
)

all_vars <- c(outcome, exposure, pollutants, confounders)
df_cc <- df2[complete.cases(df2[, all_vars]), ]

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))), data = d)
    )[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  # Total effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

results_list <- lapply(pollutants, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = pollutants,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### PEF_ratio_bin Mediation Results (Pollutants as Mediator)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```




Cookstove (exposure) to Pollutant (mediator) to lung_disease (outcome) mediation analysis
```{r}
library(logistf)
library(boot)
library(parallel)

n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1

confounders <- c("Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
exposure    <- "Type"
outcome     <- "lung_disease"
pollutants  <- c(
  "log_PM10_24hr", "log_PM2.5_24hr", "log_PM1_24hr", "log_CO_24hr",
  "log_PM10_c", "log_PM2.5_c", "log_PM1_c", "log_BC_c", "log_CO_c"
)

all_vars <- c(outcome, exposure, pollutants, confounders)
df_cc <- df[complete.cases(df[, all_vars]), ]   # <--- Use df instead of df2

boot_mediation_effects <- function(mediator_name, outcome_name = outcome) {
  # ACME (indirect effect)
  boot_acme <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    beta_tm <- coef(
      lm(as.formula(paste(mediator_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))), data = d)
    )[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    beta_my <- fit_y$coefficients[mediator_name]
    beta_tm * beta_my
  }
  # ADE (direct effect)
  boot_ade <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", mediator_name, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  # Total effect
  boot_total <- function(data, inds) {
    d <- data[inds, ]
    fit_y <- logistf(
      formula = as.formula(paste(outcome_name, "~", exposure, "+", paste(confounders, collapse = "+"))),
      data    = d,
      control = logistf.control(maxit = 200, maxstep = 100),
      plcontrol = logistpl.control(maxit = 200)
    )
    fit_y$coefficients[paste0(exposure, "Improved")]
  }
  set.seed(2025)
  boot_acme_obj  <- boot(df_cc, boot_acme,  R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_ade_obj   <- boot(df_cc, boot_ade,   R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  boot_total_obj <- boot(df_cc, boot_total, R = 5000, parallel = "multicore", ncpus = n_cores)
  ci_acme  <- boot.ci(boot_acme_obj,  type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_ade   <- boot.ci(boot_ade_obj,   type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  ci_total <- boot.ci(boot_total_obj, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  c(
    ACME  = mean(boot_acme_obj$t),
    ACME_L = ci_acme[1], ACME_U = ci_acme[2],
    ADE   = mean(boot_ade_obj$t),
    ADE_L = ci_ade[1],  ADE_U = ci_ade[2],
    TOTAL = mean(boot_total_obj$t),
    TOTAL_L = ci_total[1], TOTAL_U = ci_total[2]
  )
}

results_list <- lapply(pollutants, boot_mediation_effects)

results_df <- data.frame(
  Mediator    = pollutants,
  ACME        = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME"]),
  ACME_Lower  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_L"]),
  ACME_Upper  = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ACME_U"]),
  ADE         = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE"]),
  ADE_Lower   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_L"]),
  ADE_Upper   = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["ADE_U"]),
  Total       = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL"]),
  Total_Lower = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_L"]),
  Total_Upper = sapply(results_list, function(x) x["TOTAL_U"])
)

cat("### Lung Disease Mediation Results (Pollutants as Mediator)\n")
print(
  format(
    results_df,
    digits = 3,
    nsmall = 3,
    justify = "centre"
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)


```




8/4/2025
FEV ratio outcome linear regression and quartile/quantile
```{r}
# Count below and above 80
count_summary <- function(x) {
  below <- sum(x < 80, na.rm = TRUE)
  above <- sum(x >= 80, na.rm = TRUE)
  return(c(below = below, above = above))
}

fvc_count <- count_summary(df$FVC_ratio)
pef_count <- count_summary(df$PEF_ratio)



library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(x = seq_along(FVC_ratio), y = FVC_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of FVC_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "FVC_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(df, aes(x = seq_along(FVC_ratio), y = FVC_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of FVC_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "FVC_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(df, aes(x = seq_along(PEF_ratio), y = PEF_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of PEF_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "PEF_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

```



```{r}
# Helper function: counts and filtered data (returns a list)
count_summary_and_filter <- function(x) {
  x_clean <- x[!is.na(x)]
  below <- sum(x_clean < 80)
  above <- sum(x_clean >= 80)
  list(x = x_clean, counts = c(below = below, above = above))
}

# Prepare filtered data and counts for each variable
fvc      <- count_summary_and_filter(df$FVC_ratio)
pef      <- count_summary_and_filter(df$PEF_ratio)
fev1     <- count_summary_and_filter(df$FEV1_ratio)

library(ggplot2)

# FVC_ratio plot (NAs filtered)
ggplot(data.frame(FVC_ratio = fvc$x), aes(x = seq_along(FVC_ratio), y = FVC_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 5, y = max(fvc$x) + 5,
           label = paste("Below 80:", fvc$counts["below"], "\nAbove 80:", fvc$counts["above"]),
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of FVC_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "FVC_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

# PEF_ratio plot (NAs filtered)
ggplot(data.frame(PEF_ratio = pef$x), aes(x = seq_along(PEF_ratio), y = PEF_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 5, y = max(pef$x) + 5,
           label = paste("Below 80:", pef$counts["below"], "\nAbove 80:", pef$counts["above"]),
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of PEF_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "PEF_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

# FEV1_ratio plot (NAs filtered)
ggplot(data.frame(FEV1_ratio = fev1$x), aes(x = seq_along(FEV1_ratio), y = FEV1_ratio)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 5, y = max(fev1$x) + 5,
           label = paste("Below 80:", fev1$counts["below"], "\nAbove 80:", fev1$counts["above"]),
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of FEV1_ratio with Reference Line at 80",
       x = "Observation Index", y = "FEV1_ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
df$lung_disease <- ifelse(
  is.na(df$FVC_ratio) | is.na(df$FEV1_ratio) | is.na(df$PEF_ratio),
  NA,
  ifelse(
    df$FVC_ratio >= 80 & df$FEV1_ratio >= 80 & df$PEF_ratio >= 80,
    0,  # No lung disease
    1   # Lung disease present (any < 80)
  )
)
table(df$lung_disease)

prop.table(table(df$lung_disease))
locked_conf <- c("Type", "Electricity", "focus_group", "indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
other_vars  <- c("Education_Grouped", "Roof_Category",
                 "HH_Size_Grouped")
res_LRT_locked_lung_disease <- backward_selection_LRT(
  df                = df,            # use the complete‐cases cough data frame
  outcome           = "lung_disease",     # new binary outcome
  predictors        = other_vars,
  locked_predictors = locked_vars,
  p_cut             = 0.20,
  family            = binomial
)

# 3) Inspect what was dropped
cat("\nDropped terms:\n")
print(res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$dropped)

# 4) View the final model summary
cat("\n=== Final Model Summary ===\n")
print(summary(res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$model))
```



check assumption
```{r}
check_logistic_assumptions(model = res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$model) 
```


Lung Disease Multivariate model (each pollutant + non health variable)
```{r}
# Make sure these variables already exist in your dataframe as log-transformed versions!
# i.e., df$log_PM10_24hr, df$log_PM2.5_24hr, ..., df$log_CO_c

# 1. PM10_24hr (log)
m1 <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
            indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + log_PM10_24hr,
          data = df, family = binomial)
check_logistic_assumptions(model = m1) 




# Create a copy of df for quartile analysis
df_quart <- df

# Create quartile variable for PM10_24hr
df_quart$PM10_24hr_quartile <- cut(df_quart$PM10_24hr,
                                   breaks = quantile(df_quart$PM10_24hr, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE),
                                   include.lowest = TRUE,
                                   labels = c("Q1 (lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (highest)"))

# Fit the quartile model
m1_quartile <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                     indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + PM10_24hr_quartile,
                   data = df_quart, family = binomial)

check_logistic_assumptions(model = m1_quartile) 


# Descriptive table by PM10 quartile
library(tableone)
vars <- c("Type","indoor_outdoor_kitchen","Electricity","Education_Grouped","focus_group")
CreateTableOne(vars = vars, strata = "PM10_24hr_quartile", data = df_quart)


# Trend test model: quartile as numeric
df_quart$pm10_q_num <- as.numeric(df_quart$PM10_24hr_quartile)  # 1..4
m1_trend <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                  indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + pm10_q_num,
                data = df_quart, family = binomial)
summary(m1_trend)  # p>0.05 → no evidence of trend
```
```{r}
# 2. PM2.5_24hr (log)
m2 <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
            indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + log_PM2.5_24hr,
          data = df, family = binomial)
check_logistic_assumptions(model = m2) 


# Create a copy of df for quartile analysis
df_quart <- df

# Create quartile variable in the new data frame
df_quart$PM25_24hr_quartile <- cut(df_quart$PM2.5_24hr,
                                   breaks = quantile(df_quart$PM2.5_24hr, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE),
                                   include.lowest = TRUE,
                                   labels = c("Q1 (lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (highest)"))

# Fit the quartile model
m2_quartile <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                     indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + PM25_24hr_quartile,
                   data = df_quart, family = binomial)

check_logistic_assumptions(model = m2_quartile) 

library(tableone)
vars <- c("Type","indoor_outdoor_kitchen","Electricity","Education_Grouped","focus_group")
CreateTableOne(vars = vars, strata = "PM25_24hr_quartile", data = df_quart)

df_quart$pm25_q_num <- as.numeric(df_quart$PM25_24hr_quartile)   # 1..4
m2_trend <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                  indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + pm25_q_num,
                data = df_quart, family = binomial)
summary(m2_trend)  # if p>0.05, no evidence of trend


```




```{r}
# 3. PM1_24hr (log)
m3 <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
            indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + log_PM1_24hr,
          data = df, family = binomial)
check_logistic_assumptions(model = m3) 











# -------- Quartile version (new data.frame) -----------------------------------
df_pm1q <- df

df_pm1q$PM1_24hr_quartile <- cut(
  df_pm1q$PM1_24hr,
  breaks = quantile(df_pm1q$PM1_24hr, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = TRUE),
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c("Q1 (lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (highest)")
)

# Fit quartile model
m3_quartile <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                     indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + PM1_24hr_quartile,
                   data = df_pm1q, family = binomial)

# Assumptions check for quartile model
check_logistic_assumptions(model = m3_quartile)

# ORs & 95% CIs (vs Q1)
exp(cbind(OR = coef(m3_quartile), confint(m3_quartile)))

# -------- Balance across PM1 quartiles ----------------------------------------
library(tableone)
vars <- c("Type","indoor_outdoor_kitchen","Electricity","Education_Grouped","focus_group")
CreateTableOne(vars = vars, strata = "PM1_24hr_quartile", data = df_pm1q)

# -------- Ordinal trend test (1..4) -------------------------------------------
df_pm1q$pm1_q_num <- as.numeric(df_pm1q$PM1_24hr_quartile)  # 1,2,3,4

m3_trend <- glm(lung_disease ~ Type + Electricity + focus_group +
                  indoor_outdoor_kitchen + Education_Grouped + pm1_q_num,
                data = df_pm1q, family = binomial)
summary(m3_trend)   # if p > 0.05 -> no monotonic trend
```

Manuscript Writing Graph (from Pollutant ~ Non-health)
```{r}
# --- Figure 3: Exposure reductions with IDES vs Traditional --------------------
library(tidyverse)

# helper: pull the 'Type' coefficient (IDES vs Traditional) from a selected-model result
extract_pct_for_type <- function(res_obj, pollutant_label, window_label) {
  m <- res_obj$model
  # find the coefficient whose name starts with 'Type'
  type_term <- grep("^Type", names(coef(m)), value = TRUE)[1]
  if (is.na(type_term)) {
    stop(paste0("No coefficient starting with 'Type' in model for ", pollutant_label))
  }
  beta <- coef(m)[type_term]
  se   <- sqrt(vcov(m)[type_term, type_term])
  lwr  <- beta - 1.96 * se
  upr  <- beta + 1.96 * se
  
  tibble(
    Pollutant   = pollutant_label,
    TimeWindow  = window_label,
    term        = type_term,
    beta        = beta,
    se          = se,
    pct_change  = (exp(beta) - 1) * 100,
    pct_l       = (exp(lwr)  - 1) * 100,
    pct_u       = (exp(upr)  - 1) * 100
  )
}

# build data for the two facets using YOUR objects
fig3_24 <- bind_rows(
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM10_24hr_result, "PM10",  "24-hr"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM2.5_24hr_result, "PM2.5", "24-hr"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM1_24hr_result,  "PM1",   "24-hr"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_CO_24hr_result,   "CO",    "24-hr")
)

fig3_cook <- bind_rows(
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM10_c_result, "PM10 (cooking)",  "Cooking"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM2.5_c_result, "PM2.5 (cooking)", "Cooking"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_PM1_c_result,  "PM1 (cooking)",   "Cooking"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_BC_c_result,   "BC (cooking)",    "Cooking"),
  extract_pct_for_type(log_CO_c_result,   "CO (cooking)",    "Cooking")
)

fig3_df <- bind_rows(fig3_24, fig3_cook) %>%
  group_by(TimeWindow) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(pct_change)), .by_group = TRUE) %>%   # order by magnitude within facet
  mutate(Pollutant = factor(Pollutant, levels = unique(Pollutant))) %>%
  ungroup()

# plot
library(ggplot2)
gg_fig3 <- ggplot(fig3_df, aes(x = pct_change, y = Pollutant)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pct_l, xmax = pct_u), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ TimeWindow, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Exposure reductions with IDES vs Traditional",
    x = "Adjusted percent difference (%)\n(negative = lower concentration in IDES)",
    y = NULL,
    caption = paste(
      "Models include four forced covariates (Type, Electricity, Focus village, Kitchen location)",
      "and retain other non-health predictors via backward selection (p ≤ 0.20).",
      "%Δ = (exp(β) − 1) × 100; 95% CI from model-based SEs.",
      "Refs: Traditional, No electricity, Regular village, Indoor kitchen.", sep = "\n"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

gg_fig3
ggsave(file.path("results", "Figure3_exposure_reductions_IDES.png"), gg_fig3, width = 7.2, height = 5.8, dpi = 300)

```



```{r}
# --- Figure 3 (ASCII labels; no Unicode, no X11 needed) -----------------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(purrr); library(broom)
  library(forcats); library(ggplot2)
})

mdl_list <- list(
  "PM10"   = list(obj = log_PM10_24hr_result$model,  panel = "24-hr"),
  "PM2.5"  = list(obj = log_PM2.5_24hr_result$model, panel = "24-hr"),
  "PM1"    = list(obj = log_PM1_24hr_result$model,   panel = "24-hr"),
  "CO"     = list(obj = log_CO_24hr_result$model,    panel = "24-hr"),
  "PM10_c" = list(obj = log_PM10_c_result$model,     panel = "Cooking"),
  "PM2.5_c"= list(obj = log_PM2.5_c_result$model,    panel = "Cooking"),
  "PM1_c"  = list(obj = log_PM1_c_result$model,      panel = "Cooking"),
  "BC_c"   = list(obj = log_BC_c_result$model,       panel = "Cooking"),
  "CO_c"   = list(obj = log_CO_c_result$model,       panel = "Cooking")
)

get_type_row <- function(mdl, label, panel, term_name = "TypeImproved") {
  tt <- broom::tidy(mdl, conf.int = TRUE)
  if (!term_name %in% tt$term)
    return(tibble(pollutant = label, panel = panel,
                  pct = NA_real_, lwr = NA_real_, upr = NA_real_))
  tt %>% filter(term == term_name) %>%
    transmute(pollutant = label, panel = panel,
              pct = (exp(estimate)-1)*100,
              lwr = (exp(conf.low)-1)*100,
              upr = (exp(conf.high)-1)*100)
}

plot_df <- purrr::imap_dfr(mdl_list, ~get_type_row(.x$obj, .y, .x$panel))

label_ascii <- c(
  "PM10"="PM10", "PM2.5"="PM2.5", "PM1"="PM1", "CO"="CO",
  "PM10_c"="PM10 (cooking)", "PM2.5_c"="PM2.5 (cooking)",
  "PM1_c"="PM1 (cooking)", "BC_c"="BC (cooking)", "CO_c"="CO (cooking)"
)

plot_df_use <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(panel = factor(panel, levels = c("24-hr","Cooking")),
         pollutant = forcats::fct_rev(forcats::fct_inorder(label_ascii[pollutant])))

x_min <- floor(min(plot_df$lwr, na.rm = TRUE)/10)*10
x_max <- 0

p <- ggplot(plot_df_use, aes(x = pct, y = pollutant)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4, colour = "grey35") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lwr, xmax = upr), height = 0, linewidth = 0.8, colour = "grey20") +
  geom_point(size = 2.2, shape = 16) +
  facet_grid(panel ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(x_min, x_max),
                     breaks = seq(x_min, x_max, by = 10),
                     labels = function(x) sprintf("%d%%", x)) +
  labs(title = "Exposure reductions with IDES vs traditional stoves",
       x = "Adjusted percent difference (IDES relative to traditional)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=12, hjust=0),
        strip.text.y.left = element_text(face="bold", angle=0, margin = margin(r=8)),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color="grey88", linewidth=0.3),
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t=8)),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(6,10,6,6))

print(p)
ggsave(file.path("results", "Figure3_IDES_reductions.pdf"), p, width=6.5, height=5, units="in", dpi=300)
ggsave(file.path("results", "Figure3_IDES_reductions.png"), p, width=6.5, height=5, units="in", dpi=300)


```

```{r}
# --- Figure 4 (Base models; 5 outcomes) — FIXED variable names ---------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(purrr); library(broom)
  library(forcats); library(ggplot2); library(cowplot)
})

# Use your existing specs if already defined; otherwise set them here
if (!exists("locked_vars", inherits = TRUE)) {
  locked_vars <- c("Type","Electricity","focus_group","indoor_outdoor_kitchen")
}
if (!exists("other_vars", inherits = TRUE)) {
  other_vars <- c("Education_Grouped","Income_Grouped","Roof_Category",
                  "Kitchen_Ventilation_Combination","HH_Size_Grouped")
}

# Helper: fit base logistic model (four locks + selection p<=0.20), then tidy ORs for 4 locks
fit_base_and_tidy <- function(data, outcome_var, pretty_label) {
  res <- backward_selection_LRT(
    df                = data,
    outcome           = outcome_var,                 # <— real column name
    predictors        = c(locked_vars, other_vars),
    locked_predictors = locked_vars,
    p_cut             = 0.20,
    family            = binomial()
  )

  tt <- broom::tidy(res$model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE)

  # Keep the 4 locked terms using regex (robust to small name differences)
  tt_locked <- tt %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      grepl("^Type", term) |
      grepl("^Electricity", term) |
      grepl("^(focus_group|Focus_Group)", term) |
      grepl("^indoor_outdoor_kitchen", term)
    ) %>%
    # If a factor has multiple non-reference levels, take the first occurrence
    dplyr::group_by(grp = dplyr::case_when(
      grepl("^Type", term) ~ "Type (IDES vs Traditional)",
      grepl("^Electricity", term) ~ "Electricity (Has vs No)",
      grepl("^(focus_group|Focus_Group)", term) ~ "Focus village (Focus vs Regular)",
      grepl("^indoor_outdoor_kitchen", term) ~ "Kitchen (Outdoor vs Indoor)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      outcome   = pretty_label,     # <— pretty label only for plotting
      covariate = grp,
      OR  = estimate,
      LCL = conf.low,
      UCL = conf.high,
      p   = p.value
    )

  tt_locked
}

# Build tidy OR table across the five base models (use your cleaned data frames)
or_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  fit_base_and_tidy(df_wh,           "Wheezing",          "Wheeze"),
  fit_base_and_tidy(df_cc2,          "cough_binary",      "Cough"),
  fit_base_and_tidy(df_cc_phlegm,    "phlegm_binary",     "Phlegm"),
  fit_base_and_tidy(df_cc_breath,    "breathless_binary", "Breathlessness"),
  fit_base_and_tidy(df,              "lung_disease",      "Lung disease")
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("Wheeze","Cough","Phlegm","Breathlessness","Lung disease")),
    covariate = factor(covariate,
      levels = c("Type (IDES vs Traditional)",
                 "Electricity (Has vs No)",
                 "Focus village (Focus vs Regular)",
                 "Kitchen (Outdoor vs Indoor)"))
  )

# Dynamic x-limits to handle wide CIs
xmin <- max(0.2, min(or_tbl$LCL[is.finite(or_tbl$LCL)], na.rm = TRUE))
xmax <- max(   8, max(or_tbl$UCL[is.finite(or_tbl$UCL)], na.rm = TRUE))
brks <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64)
brks <- brks[brks >= xmin & brks <= max(xmax, 8)]

# Plot: outcomes on Y; one column per covariate
p4 <- ggplot(or_tbl, aes(x = OR, y = outcome)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4, colour = "grey35") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LCL, xmax = UCL), height = 0, linewidth = 0.8, colour = "grey20") +
  geom_point(size = 2.2, shape = 16) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = brks, labels = as.character(brks),
                limits = c(min(brks), max(brks))) +
  facet_grid(. ~ covariate, switch = "x") +
  labs(
    title = "Adjusted odds ratios for health outcomes (base models)",
    x = "Odds ratio (log scale)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=12, hjust=0),
    strip.text.x = element_text(face="bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color="grey88", linewidth=0.3),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 8)),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(6,10,6,6)
  )

# Show in RStudio
print(p4)

# Optional caption panel under the plot
cap_txt <- paste(
  "Base models include four forced covariates (Type, Electricity, Focus village, Kitchen location).",
  "Additional non-health predictors were retained by backward selection (p ≤ 0.20); only the four forced covariates are shown.",
  "Points = adjusted OR; bars = 95% CI; vertical line = OR = 1.", sep = "\n"
)

p4_final <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p4,
  ggdraw() + draw_label(cap_txt, x = 0, hjust = 0, size = 8, color = "grey25", lineheight = 1.1),
  ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(1, 0.18)
)

print(p4_final)
# ggsave(file.path("results", "Figure4_base_models_ORs.pdf"), p4_final, width = 7, height = 5.5, units = "in", dpi = 300)
# ggsave(file.path("results", "Figure4_base_models_ORs.png"), p4_final, width = 7, height = 5.5, units = "in", dpi = 300)


```




```{r}
# ===== Table: ALL terms that remain in each final base model (no refit) =====
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(broom); library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(purrr); library(knitr)
})

# 1) Collect your fitted models (exactly as created by your code)
model_list <- list()

if (exists("res_LRT_locked") && !is.null(res_LRT_locked$model))
  model_list[["Wheeze"]] <- res_LRT_locked$model

if (exists("res_LRT_locked_cough") && !is.null(res_LRT_locked_cough$model))
  model_list[["Cough"]] <- res_LRT_locked_cough$model

if (exists("res_LRT_locked_phlegm") && !is.null(res_LRT_locked_phlegm$model))
  model_list[["Phlegm"]] <- res_LRT_locked_phlegm$model

if (exists("res_LRT_locked_breath") && !is.null(res_LRT_locked_breath$model))
  model_list[["Breathlessness"]] <- res_LRT_locked_breath$model

if (exists("res_LRT_locked_lung_disease") && !is.null(res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$model))
  model_list[["Lung disease"]] <- res_LRT_locked_lung_disease$model

if (length(model_list) == 0L) stop("No fitted models found in the session.")

# 2) Small normalization to avoid duplicate rows from trivial name variants
normalize_term <- function(x) {
  # unify only known variants; keep everything else unchanged
  x <- sub("^ElectricityHas\\.Electricity$", "ElectricityHas Electricity", x)
  x
}

# (Optional) put locked terms first in the final sort
locked_terms <- c("TypeImproved", "ElectricityHas Electricity", "focus_groupFocus", "indoor_outdoor_kitchenOutdoor")

# 3) Tidy each model -> OR (95% CI) for ALL terms except intercept
tidy_all <- purrr::imap_dfr(model_list, function(mod, outcome_name) {
  broom::tidy(mod, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      term      = normalize_term(.data$term),
      Outcome   = outcome_name,
      OR_CI     = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f–%.2f)", .data$estimate, .data$conf.low, .data$conf.high),
      p         = .data$p.value
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(.data$Outcome, term = .data$term, .data$OR_CI, .data$p)
})

# 4) Build the union of terms across all models
all_terms <- tidy_all %>% dplyr::distinct(.data$term) %>% dplyr::pull(.data$term)

# 5) Ensure every (term, outcome) cell exists; fill missing with em-dash
skeleton <- tidyr::expand_grid(term = all_terms, Outcome = names(model_list))

tbl <- skeleton %>%
  dplyr::left_join(tidy_all, by = c("term","Outcome")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    OR_CI = ifelse(is.na(.data$OR_CI), "—", .data$OR_CI),
    sig   = ifelse(is.na(.data$p), FALSE, .data$p < 0.05),
    OR_CI = ifelse(.data$sig, paste0(.data$OR_CI, " *"), .data$OR_CI)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-.data$sig, -.data$p)

# 6) Order: locked terms first (if present), then the rest alphabetically
tbl <- tbl %>%
  dplyr::mutate(order =
    dplyr::case_when(
      .data$term == locked_terms[1] ~ 1,
      .data$term == locked_terms[2] ~ 2,
      .data$term == locked_terms[3] ~ 3,
      .data$term == locked_terms[4] ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ 5
    )
  )

tbl_wide <- tbl %>%
  dplyr::arrange(.data$order, .data$term) %>%
  dplyr::select(-.data$order) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = .data$Outcome, values_from = .data$OR_CI) %>%
  dplyr::rename(`Term (model coefficient)` = .data$term)

# 7) Add N per outcome (footnote)
Ns <- purrr::map_int(model_list, stats::nobs)
N_note <- paste0("Ns: ", paste(paste0(names(Ns), "=", Ns), collapse = "; "), ".")

# 8) Print table
knitr::kable(
  tbl_wide,
  align   = "lccccc",
  caption = "Table 3. Final selected covariates from base logistic models: OR (95% CI) by outcome. Asterisk denotes p < 0.05."
)

cat("\n", N_note,
    "\nReference levels follow the model coding in your data (e.g., Traditional stove, No electricity, Regular village, Indoor kitchen, and baseline levels for categorical groups).",
    "\nModels selected via backward elimination (retention p \u2264 0.20) with four a priori covariates forced in; table displays **all** terms remaining in each final model.\n",
    sep = " ")

```


Outcome Percentage
```{r}
# ==== Outcome prevalence (0 vs 1) for each fitted base model ====
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(purrr); library(tibble); library(knitr)
})

stopifnot(exists("model_list"), length(model_list) > 0)

outcome_prevalence <- imap_dfr(model_list, function(mod, outcome_name) {
  mf <- stats::model.frame(mod)
  yname <- colnames(mf)[1]
  y <- mf[[1]]

  zero_lab <- "0"; one_lab <- "1"
  # Coerce to 0/1 and record labels if factor/logical
  if (is.factor(y) && nlevels(y) == 2) {
    levs <- levels(y)
    zero_lab <- levs[1]
    one_lab  <- levs[2]
    ybin <- as.integer(y == levs[2]) # treat 2nd level as 1
  } else if (is.logical(y)) {
    zero_lab <- "FALSE"; one_lab <- "TRUE"
    ybin <- as.integer(y)
  } else {
    # assume already 0/1 numeric/integer
    ybin <- as.integer(y)
  }

  n_tot <- sum(!is.na(ybin))
  n1 <- sum(ybin == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n0 <- sum(ybin == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  tibble(
    Outcome      = outcome_name,
    Outcome_var  = yname,
    `n (total)`  = n_tot,
    `n0`         = n0,
    `n1`         = n1,
    `0 %`        = round(100 * n0 / n_tot, 1),
    `1 %`        = round(100 * n1 / n_tot, 1),
    `0 label`    = zero_lab,
    `1 label`    = one_lab
  )
})

kable(
  outcome_prevalence,
  align = "lrrrrrrll",
  caption = "Outcome prevalence (0 vs 1) in the analysis data used for each base logistic model."
)

```








```{R}
# Packages to report
pkgs <- c("cobalt","survey","EValue","broom","dplyr","ggplot2",
          "mediation","medflex","geepack","lme4")

pv <- function(pkg) {
  tryCatch(as.character(utils::packageVersion(pkg)), error = function(e) NA_character_)
}

vers <- vapply(pkgs, pv, "")
keep <- !is.na(vers) & nzchar(vers)
items <- paste0(pkgs[keep], " ", vers[keep])

# Fill if you actually bootstrapped
SEED <- NA_integer_   # e.g., 202510
B    <- NA_integer_   # e.g., 2000

rver     <- R.version.string
platform <- R.version$platform
osname   <- Sys.info()[["sysname"]]

pkg_line <- paste(items, collapse = "; ")

seed_line <- if (!is.na(SEED) && !is.na(B)) {
  sprintf(" If bootstrap was used for mediation uncertainty, the random number generator was initialized with set.seed(%d) and %d bootstrap replicates were drawn.", SEED, B)
} else {
  ""
}

methods_para <- sprintf(
  "Analyses were conducted in %s (%s, %s); key packages at analysis time included %s. For reproducibility, a session snapshot was saved with sessionInfo().%s",
  rver, platform, osname, pkg_line, seed_line
)

cat(methods_para, "\n")


```
